DJ4: Theater SIGINT System                 -      3.660          -             -           -           -           -           -          -            -         0.000      3.660
(TSIGS)

A. Mission Description and Budget Item Justification
This Program Element focuses on Theater SIGINT Systems (TSIGS) which provides Army commanders with a forward deployable, remote or locally controlled, signals
intelligence (SIGINT) system for contingency operations. TSIGS, made up of the Trojan Soldier Portable Remote Intelligence Group (TSPRING) and Picketline, are
currently in the field.

Project DJ4 supports the continuous software sustainment of Theater SIGINT System (TSIGS) which is comprised of Persistent (fixed site), Non-persistent (mobile), and
Survey (man portable) subsystems. TSIGS provides SIGINT integrated solutions to support Multi Domain Battle capability gaps and provide force protection, situational
development, and information superiority to Army Service Component Commands (ASCCs) and Combatant Commands (COCOMs) at the strategic level. TSIGS equips
Military Intelligence Brigades with non-persistent mobile capabilities, persistent static capabilities, and portable survey capabilities to their subordinate formations.
Enables integration and interoperability with sustained capabilities in support of theater aligned Military Intelligence units and Intelligence Community (IC) collection
priorities, as well as Transformation in Contact (TiC) initiatives, Presidential Directives and Operational Needs Statements.

(TSIGS)
Quantity of RDT&E Articles                   -         -          -           -           -           -             -             -             -            -

A. Mission Description and Budget Item Justification
This funding line will provide SIGINT integrated solutions to support Multi Domain Battle capability gaps and provide force protection, situational development, and
information superiority to Army Service Component Commands and Combatant Commands at the strategic level. Theater SIGINT System (TSIGS) is comprised of
Trojan Soldier Portable Remote Intelligence Group (TSPRING), and Picketline. TSIGS information superiority provides Commanders and staffs timely, accurate,
relevant, and predictive intelligence to understand threat characteristics, goals and objectives, supporting the military decision-making process, course of action
development, and targeting support. TSIGS employs technologically advanced systems with a Modular Open-System Approach (MOSA) in multiple configurations
that can be efficiently sustained to provide capabilities against evolving peer, near peer and emerging threats. Enables integration and interoperability with sustained
capabilities in support of theater aligned Military Intelligence units and Intelligence Community (IC) collection priorities, as well as Transformation in Contact (TiC)
initiatives, Presidential Directives and Operational Needs Statements.

B. Accomplishments/Planned Programs ($ in Millions)                                                                                          FY 2025             FY 2026       FY 2027
Title: SW Sustainment / Operational Readiness                                                                                                    2.196                  -             -
Description: Funds enable a Continuous Integration Continuous Delivery (CI/CD) pipeline to deliver TSIGS software and
cybersecurity updates at the speed of relevancy.

Title: Technical Management Support                                                                                                                  1.464              -                -
Description: Funds will provide Technical/Program Management support for engineering, integration and test activities for the
TSIGS program.

                                                                                  Accomplishments/Planned Programs Subtotals                         3.660              -                -

D. Acquisition Strategy
The Theater SIGINT System (TSIGS) program will use a Major Capability Acquisition (MCA) tailored approach to sustain operationally and tactically relevant SIGINT
hardware/software capabilities to the Warfighter. The MCA Pathway will enable the program to continue the sustainment, integration and delivery of adaptable products.

DJ3: Joint Reduced Range                     -     13.070      28.893      16.014           -       16.014      23.051       18.031       3.823            -         0.000     102.882
Rocket

A. Mission Description and Budget Item Justification
The Joint Reduced Range Rocket (JR3) is a replacement for the Low Cost Reduced Range Practice Rocket (LCRRPR). LCRRPR is the only live training rocket that
supports the annual qualification and pre-deployment requirements for Active and National Guard High Mobility Artillery Rocket System (HIMARS) and MLRS launcher
units. JR3 is required because the remaining inventory of launch pod containers and motors is insufficient to support LCRRPR production beyond FY 2030.

 The total cost of the JR3 Middle Tier of Acquisition Rapid Prototyping (MTA RP) effort is $115.7 million RDT&E from FY 2025 to FY 2030.

 FY 2027 funding continues rapid prototyping with flight demonstrations and initiates qualification of the JR3.

 The munitions developed under this funding line are part of the MLRS Family of Munitions (MFOM) that are deployed by the M142 HIMARS and M270 MLRS launchers.

        Change Summary Explanation
        FY 2027 funding increase reflects the fact that the FY 2026 President's Budget request did not include out-year funding.




PE 0605244A: Joint Reduced Range Rocket (JR3)                                UNCLASSIFIED

                                     Years       FY 2025    FY 2026     Base         OOC          Total     FY 2028     FY 2029     FY 2030    FY 2031 Complete             Cost
DJ3: Joint Reduced Range                     -     13.070     28.893      16.014           -       16.014     23.051       18.031      3.823            -         0.000     102.882
Rocket
Quantity of RDT&E Articles                   -         -          -           -            -           -          -            -          -             -

A. Mission Description and Budget Item Justification
In order to achieve and maintain combat readiness, the Army has a Standards in Training Commission (STRAC) requirement that utilizes a training rocket to meet the
annual certification and pre-deployment requirements for Active and National Guard High Mobility Artillery Rocket System (HIMARS) and M270A1/M270A2 Multiple
Launch Rocket System (MLRS) launcher units. The Joint Reduced Range Rocket (JR3) is required because the remaining inventory of launch pod containers and
motors is insufficient to support LCRRPR production beyond FY 2030.

 The total cost of the JR3 Middle Tier of Acquisition Rapid Prototyping (MTA RP) effort is $115.7 million RDT&E from FY 2025 to FY 2030.

 FY 2027 funding continues rapid prototyping with a flight demonstration and initiates qualification of the JR3. Upon completion of development and qualification, the JR3
 will replace the currently fielded LCRRPR.

B. Accomplishments/Planned Programs ($ in Millions)                                                                                      FY 2025            FY 2026       FY 2027
Title: Joint Reduced Range Rocket                                                                                                           13.070             28.893        16.014
Description: The JR3 program will develop a new training rocket that can be fired by crews on the HIMARS and MLRS launchers.

FY 2026 Plans:
Continue development of JR3 Prototype rockets and pods to support design reviews and ground testing of components and
systems to support prototype flight demonstrations starting in FY 2026.
FY 2027 Plans:
Complete competitive demonstration activities and begin transition to qualification and testing in support of replacing the
LCRRPR.
FY 2026 to FY 2027 Increase/Decrease Statement:
Decrease in FY 2027 due to completion of competitive prototyping phase and transition to qualification phase.
                                                                                   Accomplishments/Planned Programs Subtotals                  13.070         28.893        16.014

C. Other Program Funding Summary ($ in Millions)
N/A


PE 0605244A: Joint Reduced Range Rocket (JR3)                               UNCLASSIFIED
                                                                                                                                                                  Volume 3d - 102
Army                                                                               Page 2 of 6                         R-1 Line #159

===== PAGE 135 =====
                                                                            UNCLASSIFIED
Exhibit R-2A, RDT&E Project Justification: PB 2027 Army                                                                                    Date: April 2026
Appropriation/Budget Activity                                                    R-1 Program Element (Number/Name)      Project (Number/Name)
2040 / 5                                                                         PE 0605244A / Joint Reduced Range Rock DJ3 / Joint Reduced Range Rocket
                                                                                 et (JR3)
C. Other Program Funding Summary ($ in Millions)
Remarks

D. Acquisition Strategy
JR3 is a Middle Tier of Acquisition Rapid Prototyping effort that will develop and qualify a replacement for the LCRRPR. JR3 must begin production by FY 2030 to
support HIMARS and MLRS crew training requirements. LCRRPR utilizes solid rocket motors last built in the mid-90s and inventories will be depleted by FY 2030
with no ability to replenish using current assets. The JR3 program shall leverage available rocket motor technologies to develop a new training rocket that results in
a configuration that is affordable and supports the required production timeline. The JR3 rocket shall be integrated into a launch pod container (LPC) that shall be
compatible with current HIMARS and MLRS launchers. The JR3 effort is being executed as an Other Transactional Authority (OTA) initiated in FY 2025 to conduct
competitive prototyping of up to three candidate systems culminating in prototype flight testing and down-select to a single solution in FY 2027. A follow-on qualification
phase initiates in FY 2027 to inform a future production decision.

                                   Prior                            FY 2027        FY 2027      FY 2027                                                   Cost To       Total

Total Program Element                      -      4.495         -             -           -           -           -           -          -            -   Continuing Continuing
DJ8: Spectrum Situational                  -      4.495         -             -           -           -           -           -          -            -   Continuing Continuing
Awareness System (S2AS)

Note
In FY 2026, Program Element (PE) 0605247A Spectrum Situational Awareness System (S2AS), Project Code DJ8 Spectrum Situational Awareness System (S2AS) was
transferred to Budget Activity (BA) 5 Agile RDTE Portfolio Management under PE 0605275A Electronic Warfare Systems Development, Project Code A98 Spectrum
Situational Awareness System (S2AS).

A. Mission Description and Budget Item Justification
Spectrum Situational Awareness System (S2AS) supports Army key modernization initiatives by allowing commanders to sense and see themselves in the Electro-
Magnetic Spectrum (EMS), preventing electronic fratricide, helping shape the electronic operating environment, and increasing Command Post (CP) survivability.
Operationally S2AS allows commanders to determine and impact their visibility to enemy fire chain. S2AS is a dedicated EMS situational awareness management
system that provides the Commander with real time EMS Situational Awareness to support Emissions Control (EMCON) decisions, Electromagnetic Interference (EMI)
Resolution, and detect/warn operations centers of unauthorized or intentional sources of interference on today's complex battlefield. S2AS will employ sophisticated
hardware and software to strengthen the capabilities of the Army and ensuring resilience against potential adversarial threat. S2AS will address current and future
EMS challenges, ensuring awareness and secure use of the EMS. Enables integration, interoperability and force modernization with emerging capabilities in support of
USAREUR-AF and USARPAC Operational Needs Statements, Transformation in Contact (TIC) initiatives, and Presidential Directives.

     • I31012: SPECTRUM                      -            -           -            -            -           -            -           -            -
  SITUATIONAL AWARENESS
        SYSTEM (S2AS)
Remarks

D. Acquisition Strategy
The S2AS program will use a tailored competitive acquisition approach to rapidly deliver an integrated spectrum monitoring capability on multiple platform types. The
S2AS will transition early prototypes from the Terrestrial Layer System Echelons Above Brigade (TLS EAB) program to accelerate demonstration and fielding of S2AS
capability. The S2AS program will leverage authorities including but not limited to Urgent Capability Acquisition and/or Middle Tier of Acquisition to accelerate delivery,
initially focusing on capabilities for Transformation in Contact Units. Enables integration, interoperability and force modernization with emerging capabilities in support of
USAREUR-AF and USARPAC Operational Needs Statements, Transformation in Contact (TiC) initiatives, and Presidential Directives.

SITUATIONAL AWARENESS
SYSTEM (S2AS)

A. Mission Description and Budget Item Justification
The FY 2027 funding in the amount of $99.691 million supports the Electronic Warfare Agile Systems Development encompassing engineering, manufacturing, and
software development for systems to plan, manage, detect, interpret, and shape the Electromagnetic Spectrum (EMS) and its use by friendly and hostile forces.
The systems developed under this program provide the Army a means to see their own emissions preventing electronic fratricide, helping shape the electronic
operating environment, and increasing Soldier and Command Post (CP) survivability. This program also provides timely rapid reprogramming of mission software
supporting evolving Electronic Attack capabilities and supports information dissemination for Army supported, Joint and allied services. Together these systems enable
understanding and control of the EMS for US Forces.

 Project Code A94 - Navigation Warfare (NAVWAR) provides Commanders and Soldiers with indications and warnings to Positioning, Navigation, and Timing (PNT)
 users that their navigation and timing may be compromised by jamming or spoofing. The system will detect, identify, and locate sources of PNT interference allowing
 Command and Control (C2) systems to display areas affected by interference, and provide actionable information necessary to implement measures to mitigate or
 eliminate the threat. The overall goal of NAVWAR Attack operations is to preserve PNT overmatch for friendly forces by creating a denied, degraded and/or disrupted
 operating environment for adversaries while opening windows of PNT superiority for friendly forces. NAVWAR Attack links in Fires, Aviation, and Electronic Warfare
 communities, producing outcomes that support Army NAVWAR dominance, informing NAVWAR Situational Awareness of effects in the PNT radio frequency spectrum.
 NAVWAR Attack will streamline these efforts, providing NAVWAR Attack effects and tools to the Commander to shape the electromagnetic operating environment.
 NAVWAR Attack aligns with the Multi-Domain Operations (MDO) concept and the Army Operations Concept by providing globally responsive arms teams with the
 ability to present multiple dilemmas to the enemy, limits enemy options and exploits the enemy's weaknesses. Approved Requirements: NAVWAR Attack Abbreviated
 Capabilities Development Document (A-CDD) 09 September 2022.


PE 0605275A: Electronic Warfare Systems Development                       UNCLASSIFIED
                                                                                                                                                                Volume 3d - 113
Army                                                                           Page 1 of 31                         R-1 Line #161

===== PAGE 146 =====
                                                                           UNCLASSIFIED
Exhibit R-2, RDT&E Budget Item Justification: PB 2027 Army                                                                               Date: April 2026
Appropriation/Budget Activity                                                  R-1 Program Element (Number/Name)
2040: Research, Development, Test & Evaluation, Army / BA 5: System            PE 0605275A / Electronic Warfare Systems Development
Development & Demonstration (SDD)
 Project Code A95 / Army Reprogramming Analysis Team (ARAT): The ARAT EW enterprise supports the tactical Commander by providing timely rapid reprogramming
 of mission software and information dissemination for the Army. ARAT supports integrated reprogramming of target acquisition, target engagement, platform survivability
 systems, and Aircraft Survivability Equipment (ASE). The ARAT rapid-reprogramming EW enterprise supports tactical requirements and readiness for deployed aircraft
 and ground-based terrestrial systems. ARAT identifies and analyzes threat signature changes which affect EW systems; determines the impact of observed signature
 changes; develops new mission software solutions to adapt the system to the threat changes; disseminates the mission software; and provides methods to upload the
 new mission software into the affected EW systems.

 Project Code A96 / Integrated Electronic Warfare Systems: Electromagnetic warfare (EW) capability gaps pose growing risks to the Army operational effectiveness,
 requiring strategic investments in next-generation solutions. Investment in developing advanced countermeasures and integration of artificial intelligence with advanced
 analytics are required to maintain operational superiority, as adversaries continue to advance their own EW technologies and tactics. Funding is intended to support
 the development of a Next-Generation modular, scalable multi-mission. EW suite of capabilities for a broad range of modern radio frequency-controlled threats. Efforts
 include the development and integration of new and existing techniques, as well as targeted hardware and software upgrades to improve threat identification, reduce
 response time, and increase overall mission effectiveness. These investments directly support Transformation in Contact (TiC) objectives and comply with relevant
 Presidential Directives, ensuring alignment with broader defense modernization priorities.

 Project Code A97 / Counter Surveillance Reconnaissance (CSR): The CSR Abbreviated Capabilities Development Document (A-CDD) was signed on 8 January 2024.
 We are chartered to develop a family of terrestrial EW effectors to counter space-based surveillance threats. CSR non-kinetic effects will disrupt enemy kill chains by
 protecting and concealing friendly maneuver. CSR will provide three distinct classified counter-space surveillance Lines of Effort (LOE) and mission planner software
 to orchestrate CSR non-kinetic effects. Further advocacy for CSR capabilities exists in Joint Emergent Operational Needs Statement (JEONS), Operational Needs
 Statements (ONS), and Combatant Command Integrated Priority Lists. The total cost of the Counter Surveillance Reconnaissance Middle Tier of Acquisition effort is
 $422,574 million from FY2027 to FY2031. The CSR program is fully funded across the Future Years Defense Program (FYDP).

 Project Code A98 / Spectrum Situational Awareness System (S2AS): S2AS supports Army key modernization initiatives by allowing commanders to sense and
 see themselves in the Electro- Magnetic Spectrum (EMS), preventing electronic fratricide, helping shape the electronic operating environment, and increasing
 Command Post (CP) survivability. Operationally S2AS allows commanders to determine and impact their visibility to enemy fire chain. S2AS is a dedicated EMS
 situational awareness management system that provides the Commander with real time EMS Situational Awareness to support Emissions Control (EMCON) decisions,
 Electromagnetic Interference (EMI) Resolution, and detect/warn operations centers of unauthorized or intentional sources of interference on today's complex battlefield.
 S2AS will employ sophisticated hardware and software to strengthen the capabilities of the Army and ensuring resilience against potential adversarial threats. S2AS will
 address current and future EMS challenges, ensuring awareness and secure use of the EMS.

A. Mission Description and Budget Item Justification
Adversaries are aggressively developing and fielding counter- Positioning, Navigation, and Timing (PNT) technologies that significantly reduce the Army's ability
to conduct military operations. The Army is actively pursuing Navigation Warfare (NAVWAR) Attack capabilities to produce NAVWAR solutions that leverage
Electromagnetic Warfare (EW) and Signal Intelligence (SIGINT) capabilities to Soldiers ensuring PNT overmatch by denying adversary access to PNT services.

 NAVWAR Attack provides deliberate offensive and defensive capabilities to assure friendly use and prevent adversarial exploitation of PNT information through
 coordinated employment of Space, Cyberspace, and EW capabilities. NAVWAR Attack will provide Warfighters with the ability to understand the GPS/GNSS
 environment and to produce effects across the battlefield opening windows of opportunity for PNT superiority for friendly forces against adversaries.

 NAVWAR Attack supports Multi-Domain Operations (MDO) as an enabler to precision fires, movement and maneuver, force tracking, and a host of data networks that
 tie personnel and weapon systems together into a joint or coalition force. NAVWAR enables Cyber, EW, and Space Operations Officers the ability to plan missions
 with NAVWAR Attack Planning and Targeting Software and execute missions with NAVWAR Attack Modules supporting friendly PNT superiority in multiple warfighting
 functions to include Jamming, Cyber, and Munitions.

 NAVWAR has been identified as a key enabler for multiple wartime scenarios by the 2024 EW Tabletop Exercise and mandated by the National Defense Authorization
 Act of FY24 (H.R. 2670).

across the battlefield.

Continues transition of technical engineering Science and Technology (S&T) efforts to develop a formal NAVWAR capability.

Remarks

D. Acquisition Strategy
The proposed Navigation Warfare (NAVWAR) Attack acquisition strategy will utilize competitive Commercial Solutions Opening (CSO) Other Transaction Agreements
(OTAs). The strategy will follow a Soldier-centered design process and multiple User Assessments will be used throughout the prototype and development process to
define the minimum viable product and minimum viable capability releases. The proposed strategies leverage existing commercial and government technical solutions
to enable accelerated prototyping and experimentation of systems and assess operational feedback through User Assessments of developmental solutions to validate
military utility. NAVWAR Attack is planning to leverage the transition of technical engineering Science and Technology (S&T) efforts.

2040 / 5                                                                           PE 0605275A / Electronic Warfare Systems A95 / Army Reprogramming Analysis Team

                                     Prior                             FY 2027      FY 2027      FY 2027                                                 Cost To             Total

A95: Army Reprogramming                      -         -       7.770      10.411           -       10.411     10.413       10.914      10.953       11.327         0.000      61.788
Analysis Team (ARAT)
Quantity of RDT&E Articles                   -         -           -          -           -            -          -            -           -            -

A. Mission Description and Budget Item Justification
Current military operations are conducted in a rapidly changing threat environment, where Infra-Red (IR) man-portable air defense systems (MANPADS) seekers, radar
guided surface-to-air-missiles (SAM), laser guided weapons (LGW), Counter Unmanned Aerial Systems (C-UAS), and targeting sensors are proliferating and technically
evolving. Integrated solutions are required to counter increasingly sophisticated Electromagnetic Warfare (EW) Peer and Near Peer threats. The ARAT EW enterprise
supports the tactical Commander by providing timely rapid reprogramming of mission software and information dissemination for the Army. ARAT supports integrated
reprogramming of target acquisition, target engagement, platform survivability systems, and Aircraft Survivability Equipment (ASE). The ARAT rapid-reprogramming
EW enterprise supports tactical requirements and readiness for deployed aircraft and ground-based terrestrial systems. ARAT identifies and analyzes threat signature
changes which affect EW systems; determines the impact of observed signature changes; develops new mission software solutions to adapt the system to the threat
changes; disseminates the mission software; and provides methods to upload the new mission software into the affected EW systems. Each element within the ARAT
infrastructure plays a specific role within the program's rapid reprogramming process, providing the Soldier with the capability to install mission and target identification
software at the lowest possible level, thus maximizing flexibility for tactical Commanders. ARAT participates in the operational and developmental test design of Army
EW systems and supports Joint Service Reprogramming Exercises in all theaters. ARAT Research and Development enables continuous development of: 1) automated
threat analysis tools to rapidly detect threat changes within the intelligence system, 2) tools to minimize the time to develop Mission Software and Products (MSP), 3)
tools and technology to minimize the time required to test and validate MSPs, 4) improved communications conduits to rapidly transmit mission software to upload into
supported EW systems to forward deployed combat forces. These efforts allow for rapid threat analysis, threat modeling and simulation, mission software development
and testing, distribution and uploading of mission software directly to the supported Soldier on the edge. Additionally, beginning in FY 2026 ARAT will, continue mission
support to the Terrestrial Layer System (TLS) family. ARAT will develop the capability to produce system techniques, validation testing, archiving, and distribution of
mission software to forward deployed combat forces. This program aligns with the Secretary of War's comprehensive transformation strategy and the Army Chief of
Staff's Army Transformation Initiative (ATI). Additionally, consistent with the Army's initiative to migrate to the Transformation in Contact (TiC) effort. Funding for this
program will ensure development and delivery of solutions to combat forces on the forward edge via the ARAT Electromagnetic Warfare enterprise.

B. Accomplishments/Planned Programs ($ in Millions)                                                                                       FY 2025           FY 2026        FY 2027
Title: Keeping Pace with the Enemy and Technology                                                                                                -              1.887          2.483
Description: Adversaries are exploiting and exercising emerging technologies that have the rapid capability to adjust their
weapon systems via software changes. In order for the Army to keep pace with enemies and technologies it must continuously
leverage innovation to counter threat capabilities in an expedited manner. This effort focuses on developing a capability for the
Army to rapidly develop and distribute organic mission software solutions for multiple EW systems. The Army must modernize and
enhance software tools, hardware modernization, and processes to counter enemy technologies. This Research, Development,

Title: Infrastructure Improvements Multispectral                                                                                             -        1.185           1.983
Description: Adversaries are developing and enhancing their multispectral weapon systems in attempt to achieve overmatch of
the US Forces. The enemy is employing modern technologies increase the lethality of their weapon systems being employed
against US combat platforms. This effort focuses on enhancing the Army's Multispectral Missile Warning System (MWS) software
to rapidly detect and counter effects of enemy air offensive and defensive weapons directed against US combat platforms. With
the worldwide proliferation of Radio Frequency (RF) guided missiles, Laser guided missiles, Electro Optical (EO), and Infrared
(IR) guided missiles the Army must have the capability to rapidly analyze and develop mission software solutions that detect and
counter Army Aviation platforms against this lethal threat. This effort will develop software and hardware solutions to more rapidly
detect changes in enemy threats and determine the appropriate defensive response to the multispectral threat.

FY 2026 Plans:
ARAT will execute Multi-spectral improvements and enhancements to the EW Eco System to prepare for Electronic Attack
efforts beginning in FY27. ARAT will initiate Electronic Attack activities in support of transitioning systems that support both
air and ground platforms.
FY 2027 Plans:
FY27 plans include enhancements to the Electromagnetic Warfare reprogramming enterprise.
FY 2026 to FY 2027 Increase/Decrease Statement:
FY26 to FY27 increased funding to enhance modular AI/ML test scoring in support of the EW Reprogramming Enterprise.

B. Accomplishments/Planned Programs ($ in Millions)                                                                                   FY 2025    FY 2026         FY 2027
Description: Adversaries are heavily investing in Radio Frequency (RF) Electromagnetic Warfare (EW) systems to destroy US
combat forces. US EW systems are controlled by mission software that allows friendly systems to rapidly adjust to changes in
enemy RF threat systems. The changes in enemy RF systems must be rapidly adaptive to reduce the lethality. US combat EW
systems require software that can be rapidly developed, tested, and distributed to forces on the edge. This effort focuses on
enhancing the Army's Radio Frequency (RF) Electromagnetic Warfare (EW) enterprise to rapidly respond to threat changes. The
response includes rapidly analyzing the threat, determining the response, developing and testing EW mission software solutions,
and distributing to forward deployed combat forces. For the Army to succeed and maintain overmatch it must have the capability
to rapidly respond to an ever-changing battlefield environment. Funding for this effort will enhance the Army's need to rapidly
respond to changes in adversary Radio Frequency Electromagnetic Warfare systems. Dominating the Electromagnetic Spectrum
is a combat multiplier to fight in a contested and congested EW environment.

FY 2026 Plans:
ARAT will continue with modernization efforts that support automated testing and increase capabilities for modelling and
simulation of Peer and Near Peer battlefield emitters.
FY 2027 Plans:
FY27 plans include enhancement the Electromagnetic Warfare reprogramming enterprise.
FY 2026 to FY 2027 Increase/Decrease Statement:
FY26 to FY27 increased funding to enhance modular Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning software test scoring in support of
the EW Reprogramming Enterprise.
Title: Threat Flagging and Mission Data Set Reprogramming Tool Development                                                                  -        1.653           1.873
Description: This effort focuses on enhancing the Army's capability to monitor changes in enemy Electronic Warfare systems
that affect system performance of Army detection, declaration, and countermeasure Electromagnetic Warfare (EW) systems
onboard both air and ground combat platforms. The enemy is continuously developing or modifying its EW systems. For Army
platforms to have protection against enemy systems it must have a robust capability to immediately detect changes in threat
system performance and rapidly develop, test, and distribute a mission software solutions that counter the threat. This effort will
enhance the Army's capability to bridge detection of a change in enemy threat and the rapid development of Mission Software and
Products.

FY 2026 Plans:
ARAT will continue with enhancement efforts in support of automated software testing and increased capability in modelling

Title: Arsenal Technique Validation and Worldwide Distribution                                                                            -        1.465           1.771
Description: Arsenal Technique and Validation and Worldwide Distribution is an effort that provides engineering validation of

to detect and defeat enemy weapon systems. This effort will develop automation tools to rapidly validate mission software
solutions prior to distribution to combat forces deployed worldwide. The worldwide distribution effort is a continuous effort to
enhance the Army Software Portal and Electronic Notification (ASPEN). ASPEN will continuously exercise the agile development
and Development Security Operations (DEVSECOPS) methodology to enhance performance of the portal to Soldiers conducting
combat operations on the forward edge of the battlefield where connectivity through put is reduced.

Warfare Systems
Quantity of RDT&E Articles                  -         -          -           -            -            -          -            -          -            -

A. Mission Description and Budget Item Justification
Electromagnetic warfare (EW) capability gaps pose growing risks to the Army operational effectiveness, requiring strategic investments in next-generation solutions.
Investment in developing advanced countermeasures and integration of artificial intelligence with advanced analytics are required to maintain operational superiority, as
adversaries continue to advance their own EW technologies and tactics. Funding is intended to support the development of a Next-Generation modular, scalable multi-
mission. EW suite of capabilities for a broad range of modern radio frequency-controlled threats. Efforts include the development and integration of new and existing
techniques, as well as targeted hardware and software upgrades to improve threat identification, reduce response time, and increase overall mission effectiveness.
These investments directly support Transformation in Contact (TiC) objectives and comply with relevant Presidential Directives, ensuring alignment with broader defense
modernization priorities.

B. Accomplishments/Planned Programs ($ in Millions)                                                                                      FY 2025           FY 2026        FY 2027
Title: IEWS - Integrated Electronic Warfare System                                                                                              -              1.573          1.601
Description: The Integrated Electronic Warfare System (IEWS) will provide Multi-Mission Force Protection Defensive Electronic
Attack to counter emerging Command and Control threats.

FY 2026 Plans:
Accelerate technology maturation and prototyping an integrated Multi-Mission Force Protection Defensive Electronic Attack
capability to include system hardware/software enhancements and techniques development.
FY 2027 Plans:
Continue to accelerate development efforts for a Next-Generation multi-mission EW capability to include system hardware/
software enhancements and techniques development.
FY 2026 to FY 2027 Increase/Decrease Statement:
Fiscal Year (FY) 2027 funding increase allows the level of effort anticipated to remain stable through the support of hardware and
software enhancements.
                                                                                  Accomplishments/Planned Programs Subtotals                       -           1.573          1.601

N/A

A97: Counter Surveillance                   -         -      34.405     81.038           -        81.038    120.737      119.271      33.395       33.728         0.000     422.574
Reconnaissance (CSR)
Quantity of RDT&E Articles                  -         -          -          -            -            -          -            -           -            -

A. Mission Description and Budget Item Justification
The CSR Abbreviated Capabilities Development Document (A-CDD) was signed on 8 January 2024. We are chartered to develop a family of terrestrial EW effectors to
counter space-based surveillance threats. CSR non-kinetic effects will disrupt enemy kill chains by protecting and concealing friendly maneuver. CSR will provide three
distinct classified counter-space surveillance Lines of Effort (LOE) and mission planner software to orchestrate CSR non-kinetic effects.

 The FY 2027 cost of the CSR Middle Tier of Acquisition effort is $81,038 million, including RDT&E and Development of prototypes. The Department will certify FYDP
 funding in a future budget submission.

 The total cost of the Counter Surveillance Reconnaissance Middle Tier of Acquisition effort is $422,574 million from FY2026 to FY2031. The remainder of the Counter
 Surveillance Reconnaissance program is fully funded across the Future Years Defense Program.

B. Accomplishments/Planned Programs ($ in Millions)                                                                                      FY 2025           FY 2026        FY 2027
Title: LOE 1 Prototype Development                                                                                                              -             16.500         21.955
Description: Funds will facilitate Middle Tier Acquisition Rapid Prototyping (MTA-RP) of multiple LOE 1 systems leveraging Joint
government off-the-shelf capabilities

FY 2026 Plans:
The Army plans to initiate a dual prototyping pathway, leveraging a University-Affiliated Research Center (UARC) Task Order
and Other Transaction Authority (OTA) for competitive prototyping efforts. The Army plans to accelerate development of Joint
command and control architectures/software for CSR system planning, execution, and integration through other Government/DoW
agencies.
FY 2027 Plans:
The Army will conduct laboratory and field assessments of developed LOE 1 prototypes in preparation for Rapid Fielding decision
in FY2028. The Army will continue assessment of threat data collected from LOE 1 prototypes to inform basis of issue for CSR
systems. The Army will also assess Joint mission planner investment/development for suitability for Army requirements to inform
decision to continue investment or transition to mission planner prototyping effort.
FY 2026 to FY 2027 Increase/Decrease Statement:

B. Accomplishments/Planned Programs ($ in Millions)                                                                                     FY 2025    FY 2026         FY 2027
Fiscal Year (FY) 2027 funding increase will purchase and develop up-selected vendor LOE1 prototypes for competitive
experiment and evaluation. Initial prototypes will be issued to units for Transformation in Contact (TiC) user evaluation, which will
drive additional development and test costs.
Title: LOE 2 Prototype Development                                                                                                            -        2.500           5.750
Description: Funds will support pre-prototyping investment in Navy and Army Lab efforts to refine requirements needed for
competitive prototyping effort.

FY 2026 Plans:
The Army plans to develop an initial LOE 2 prototype leveraging a Department of War (DoW) Laboratory state-of-the-art lab asset.
FY 2027 Plans:
The Army will conduct assessments of Soldier experimentation/evaluation of Joint prototypes and studies to make determination
of Army requirements in support of competitive prototyping in FY2028.
FY 2026 to FY 2027 Increase/Decrease Statement:
Fiscal Year (FY) 2027 funding increase will prioritize more mature LOEs (1 and 3) representing the gradual increase in investment
as requirements mature and the Army prepares to initiate competitive prototyping.
Title: LOE 3 Prototype Development                                                                                                            -        7.500          37.466
Description: Funds will facilitate MTA-RP of multiple LOE 3 systems.

FY 2026 Plans:
The Army plans to accelerate LOE 3 prototype development based on existing, relevant DoW and industry capabilities.
FY 2027 Plans:
The Army will continue competitive OTA with significant investment in limited vendors, to include further lab and field
demonstrations.
FY 2026 to FY 2027 Increase/Decrease Statement:
Fiscal Year (FY) 2027 funding will support the increase in LOE 3 development, including integration of hardware and software,
and enter lab and field testing.
Title: Technical/Program Management and Support                                                                                               -        5.405          11.378

The Army plans to establish the CSR Product Office, focusing on staffing and establishing technical assistance contracts to

D. Acquisition Strategy
CSR will leverage the Adaptive Acquisition Framework Middle Tier of Acquisition (MTA) Rapid Prototyping (RP) to deliver operationally and tactically relevant
sustainable hardware and software Electronic Warfare prototypes to the Warfighter. The Army will leverage available commercial and government-off-the-shelf (COTS/
GOTS) and other Joint/ Army technical solutions to enable rapid prototyping and experimentation of systems. Prototyping and Transformation in Contact (TiC: Soldier
feedback) will inform requirements, doctrine, policy, and future pathways.

SITUATIONAL AWARENESS
SYSTEM (S2AS)
Quantity of RDT&E Articles                  -         -          -          -            -            -          -            -          -            -

A. Mission Description and Budget Item Justification
Spectrum Situational Awareness System (S2AS) supports Army key modernization initiatives by allowing commanders to sense and see themselves in the Electro-
Magnetic Spectrum (EMS), preventing electronic fratricide, helping shape the electronic operating environment, and increasing Command Post (CP) survivability.
Operationally S2AS allows commanders to determine and impact their visibility to enemy fire chain. S2AS is a dedicated EMS situational awareness management
system that provides the Commander with real time EMS Situational Awareness to support Emissions Control (EMCON) decisions, Electromagnetic Interference (EMI)
Resolution, and detect/warn operations centers of unauthorized or intentional sources of interference on today's complex battlefield. S2AS will employ sophisticated
hardware and software to strengthen the capabilities of the Army and ensuring resilience against potential adversarial threats. S2AS will address current and future EMS
challenges, ensuring awareness and secure use of the EMS.

 Details provided under separate cover.

A. Mission Description and Budget Item Justification
C2 Transport 0605330A is not a new start line, it is a consolidation of MILSATCOM System Engineering (0303142 456), Unified Network Technology Trans & Integ
(UNTTI) (0604818 DD1), and Protected Tactical Satellite (0303142 CO7), CMFF Radio Cards (0604805 DH4), and Integrated Tactical Network/Enterprise Network
(0604541 BT5).

 The C2 Transport Layer supports the Army's Next Generation Command and Control (NGC2) initiative to modernize Command and Control (C2) systems. The RDT&E
 funding line consists of two portfolios: Satellite and Network Modernization (C2A) and Radio Modernization (C2B).

 The Capability Program Executive Command, Control and Information Network (CPE C2IN) is responsible for prioritizing, programming, managing and executing the
 projects detailed below and ensuring these funds are available to support the Army modernization priorities.

 Project C2A FY2027 funding supports two (2) distinct lines of effort: Protected SATCOM Resilient Anti-Jam Modem (RAM) Development and Unified Network
 Technology Transition and Integration (UNTTI).

 Protected SATCOM provides funding for continued Resilient Anti-Jam Modem (RAM) development, testing and certification of production representative modems,
 incorporating Army requirements, to support continued spiral development of critical protected communications capabilities to address resiliency in jamming
 environments. RAM provides the ability for Army tactical terminals to be resilient in a contested environment and protect against catastrophic loss of situational
 awareness and command and control during critical battle movement with Anti-Jam capabilities. RAM will provide on the move and early entry satellite terminals
 with adaptive, anti-jam communications for the highest levels of protected communications in multi domain operations and offer the tactical Army protection against
 interference that is either intentional or unintentional.

 Satellite and Network Modernization funding supports UNTTI an RDT&E initiative enabling transport agnostic, high-capacity and resilient tactical communications
 for expeditionary operations. These efforts support system/subsystem development and demonstration, aimed at integration, maturation, evaluation, and testing to
 demonstrate systems against requirements. TEM Projects, Line of Sight Modernization, SATCOM Modernization, Transport Modernization, Secure Cellular, and
 System of Systems (SoS) Training. These technologies support new and improved communications capabilities with reduced Size, Weight, and Power (SWAP),
 while increasing throughput, providing network resiliency and Low Probability of Intercept/Low Probability of Detection (LPI/LPD) capabilities. Satellite and Network
 Modernization procures, modifies, integrates, and tests system prototypes to demonstrate enhanced capabilities in accordance with Army modernization priorities. It


PE 0605330A: C2 Transport                                                  UNCLASSIFIED
                                                                                                                                                                 Volume 3d - 144
Army                                                                            Page 1 of 15                         R-1 Line #162

===== PAGE 177 =====
                                                                              UNCLASSIFIED
Exhibit R-2, RDT&E Budget Item Justification: PB 2027 Army                                                                                    Date: April 2026
Appropriation/Budget Activity                                                     R-1 Program Element (Number/Name)
2040: Research, Development, Test & Evaluation, Army / BA 5: System               PE 0605330A / C2 Transport
Development & Demonstration (SDD)
 supports developing technical, logistics, training, and other acquisition documentation to assist with the transition, insertion, and integration of efforts across PM C2
 Transport and enables the transition of developing and new technologies to support Next Generation Command and Control (NGC2).

 Project C2B - Radio Modernization: Command, Control, Communications, Computers, Cyber, Intelligence, Surveillance and Reconnaissance (C5ISR) Modular Open
 Suite of Standards (CMOSS) Mounted Form Factor (CMFF) Radio Card Mid-Tier Acquisition (MTA) Rapid Prototype (RP) program delivers Software Defined Radio
 (SDR) cards, Cryptographic Subsystem (CSS) cards, Digital Radio Heads (DRH) and Victory Audio Adapter (VAX) required by the CMOSS Mounted Form Factor
 (CMFF) Abbreviated Capability Development Document (A-CDD) for the Ground and Aviation's tactical communication requirements utilizing an Other Transaction
 Authority(OTA) contracting strategy. CMFF Radio Cards provide simultaneous transmission and reception of multiple waveforms across multiple channels in a single
 or multiple SDR card(s) integrated in the CMFF chassis with NSA certifiable CSS and interoperable with the Digital Radio Head. CMFF Radio cards initial capability
 development includes but is not limited to Single Channel Ground and Airborne Radio System (SINCGARS), Air Traffic Control (ATC), Warrior Robust Enhanced
 Network (WREN) and future advanced networking waveforms; initial Cryptographic Subsystem Card (CSS), Digital Radio Head (DRH), and Victory Audio Adapter
 (VAX). Advanced capability development includes but is not limited to Mobile User Objective System (MUOS), Link-16, High Frequency (HF), HAVEQUICK (HQ),
 Second generation Anti-jam Tactical UHF Radio for NATO (SATURN), Demand Assigned Multiple Access (DAMA) and future advanced networking waveforms; and final
 CSS, DRH and VAX. The total cost of the CMFF RC Middle Tier of Acquisition (MTA) effort is $96.1 million RDT&E from FY25 to FY29.

 FY2027 funding supports Hardware and Software Development (SDR card, CSS card, Digital Radiohead and Victory Audio Adapter), Test, Integration and Evaluation,
 and Program Management Support.

 This funding directly supports the Army's Next Generation Command and Control (NGC2) initiative, specifically targeting the modernization of the Command and Control
 (C2) Transport Layer. The C5ISR Modular Open Suite of Standards (CMOSS) Mounted Form Factor (CMFF) is a cornerstone of this effort, embodying the key tenets of
 NGC2 to deliver a more agile, interoperable, and data-centric communications backbone for ground and aviation platforms.

 Funding has been realigned into this line to support the Army's Next Generation Command and Control (NGC2) initiative.

Note
This is not a new start funding line, funds are realigned from 0303142A 456/MILSATCOM System Engineering, 0604818A DD1/Unified Network Technology Trans
& Integration (UNTTI), and 0303142A CO7/Protected Tactical Satellite Communications to C2A Satellite and Network Modernization under a new Parent program
0605330A / C2 Transport.

A. Mission Description and Budget Item Justification
Project C2A FY2027 funding supports two (2) distinct lines of effort: Protected SATCOM Resilient Anti-Jam Modem (RAM) Development and Unified Network
Technology Transition and Integration (UNTTI).

 Protected SATCOM provides funding for continued Resilient Anti-Jam Modem (RAM) development, testing and certification of production representative modems,
 incorporating Army requirements, to support continued spiral development of critical protected communications capabilities to address resiliency in jamming
 environments. RAM provides the ability for Army tactical terminals to be resilient in a contested environment and protect against catastrophic loss of situational
 awareness and command and control during critical battle movement with Anti-Jam capabilities. RAM will provide on the move and early entry satellite terminals
 with adaptive, anti-jam communications for the highest levels of protected communications in multi domain operations and offer the tactical Army protection against
 interference that is either intentional or unintentional.

 Satellite and Network Modernization funding supports Unified Network Technology Transition and Integration (UNTTI) an RDT&E initiative enabling transport agnostic,
 high-capacity and resilient tactical communications for expeditionary operations. These efforts support system/subsystem development and demonstration, aimed at
 integration, maturation, evaluation, and testing to demonstrate systems against requirements. TEM Projects, Line of Sight Modernization, SATCOM Modernization,
 Transport Modernization, Secure Cellular, Next Generation Tactical Terminal (NGTT), and System of Systems (SoS) Training. These technologies support new and
 improved communications capabilities with reduced Size, Weight, and Power (SWAP), while increasing throughput, providing network resiliency and Low Probability of
 Intercept/Low Probability of Detection (LPI/LPD) capabilities.

 Satellite and Network Modernization procures, modifies, integrates, and tests system prototypes to demonstrate enhanced capabilities in accordance with Army
 modernization priorities. It supports developing technical, logistics, training, and other acquisition documentation to assist with the transition, insertion, and integration of
 efforts across PM C2 Transport and enables the transition of developing and new technologies to support Next Generation Command and Control (NGC2). In addition,
 resources validation and test efforts improve the reliability, maintainability, and supportability of C2 Transport equipped units. These improvements avoid future costs by
 mitigating single point failures and thickening the network which ultimately improves network and cyber resiliency along with unit availability for contingency operations.

B. Accomplishments/Planned Programs ($ in Millions)                                                                                  FY 2025     FY 2026         FY 2027
Title: UNTTI - Transport Modernization, Terminals and Network Efficiency                                                                    -           -           15.526
Description: Unified Network Technology Transition & Integration effort is focused on the modernization of communications
transport technologies, increasing resiliency, data capacity/throughput, and provide enhanced capabilities in denied, degraded,
intermittent, or limited (DDIL) and Electronic Warfare (EW) communications environments, while reducing system Size, Weight,
Power, and Cost, and leveraging COTS hardware platforms both High-Throughput Low-Latency. Network Efficiency efforts are
focused to include development of simultaneous SATCOM and/or cellular links within a terminal to provide pathway diversity
to increase resiliency, data capacity/throughput, and provide enhanced capabilities in denied, degraded, intermittent, or limited
(DDIL), Electronic Warfare (EW) communications environments, leveraging COTS hardware platforms both High-Throughput Low-
Latency, and Next Generation Tactical Terminal (NGTT), exercise and experimentation. This effort also includes the software &
training that is required to implement this capability.

FY 2027 Plans:
Funds will be used for prototype development and modification of emerging technology in transport modernization terminal and
network efficiency, along with integration and testing of modernized solutions. Funds evaluation of hardware form factor variants,
supports integration and testing with various Army platforms and associated program management support.
FY 2026 to FY 2027 Increase/Decrease Statement:
Funds realigned in FY2027 from 0303142A 456/MILSATCOM System Engineering, 0604818A DD1/Unified Network Technology
Trans & Integration (UNTTI) to C2A Satellite and Network Modernization under 0605330A / C2 Transport.
Title: Protected SATCOM, Resilient Anti-Jam Modem Dev and Emerging Threat Mitigation                                                        -               -        4.729
Description: Funding supports development of a small form factor Resilient Anti-Jam Modem (RAM). This activity supports
engineering of Army requirements for PTW modems in protected tactical communications.

FY 2027 Plans:
FY2027 funds support RAM development, preparation of efforts to support Milestone C and add enduring modernization efforts
that continue system development with pursuit to reduce tech debt proactively and integrate new technologies for relevancy over
time and ensuring systems remain efficient, secure, and a capable of meeting evolving requirements and emerging threats.
FY 2026 to FY 2027 Increase/Decrease Statement:

D. Acquisition Strategy
Satellite and Network Modernization's Acquisition Strategy currently encompasses RAM development and UNTTI technology and modernization.

 RAM development (an Army only requirement) uses the successfully tested technology from the USSF A3M effort. The program leveraged an existing IDIQ contract
 established by USSF for the development of RAM and awarded a Fixed-Price Incentive Firm Target (FPIF) contract in FY2024.

 UNTTI related technologies will be pursued via competitively awarded contracts using best value source selection procedures. These technologies will be matured,
 demonstrated, tested, and evaluated in realistic environments. Selected technologies will integrate into existing programs as a modernization effort. The Integrated
 Product Team of key stakeholders including the Command and Control Cross Functional Team (C2-CFT), Command, Control, Communications, Computers, Cyber,
 Intelligence, Surveillance and Reconnaissance (C5ISR) Center, Army Capability Network (ACM) Networks and Services (N&S) determine technologies for further
 evaluation to close capability gaps.

Note
This is not a new start funding line, funds are realigned from 0604805A DH4/CMFF Radio Cards, 0604547A BT5/Integrated Tactical Network/Enterprise Network to
project C2B Radio Modernization under a new Parent program element 0605330A / C2 Transport.

A. Mission Description and Budget Item Justification
This funding directly supports the Army's Next Generation Command and Control (NGC2) initiative, specifically targeting the modernization of the Command and Control
(C2) Transport Layer. The C5ISR Modular Open Suite of Standards (CMOSS) Mounted Form Factor (CMFF) is a cornerstone of this effort, embodying the key tenets of
NGC2 to deliver a more agile, interoperable, and data-centric communications backbone for ground and aviation platforms.

 Project C2B - Radio Modernization: Command, Control, Communications, Computers, Cyber, Intelligence, Surveillance and Reconnaissance (C5ISR) Modular Open
 Suite of Standards (CMOSS) Mounted Form Factor (CMFF) Radio Card Mid-Tier Acquisition (MTA) Rapid Prototype (RP) program delivers Software Defined Radio
 (SDR) cards, Cryptographic Subsystem (CSS) cards, Digital Radio Heads (DRH) and Victory Audio Adapter (VAX) required by the CMOSS Mounted Form Factor
 (CMFF) Abbreviated Capability Development Document (A-CDD) for the Ground and Aviation's tactical communication requirements utilizing an Other Transaction
 Authority(OTA) contracting strategy. CMFF Radio Cards provide simultaneous transmission and reception of multiple waveforms across multiple channels in a single
 or multiple SDR card(s) integrated in the CMFF chassis with NSA certifiable CSS and interoperable with the Digital Radio Head. CMFF Radio cards initial capability
 development includes but is not limited to Single Channel Ground and Airborne Radio System (SINCGARS), Air Traffic Control (ATC), Warrior Robust Enhanced
 Network (WREN) and future advanced networking waveforms; initial Cryptographic Subsystem Card (CSS), Digital Radio Head (DRH), and Victory Audio Adapter
 (VAX). Advanced capability development includes but is not limited to Mobile User Objective System (MUOS), Link-16, High Frequency (HF), HAVEQUICK (HQ),
 Second generation Anti-jam Tactical UHF Radio for NATO (SATURN), Demand Assigned Multiple Access (DAMA) and future advanced networking waveforms; and final
 CSS, DRH and VAX. The total cost of the CMFF RC Middle Tier of Acquisition (MTA) effort is $96.1 million RDT&E from FY25 to FY29.

 FY2027 funding supports Hardware and Software Development (SDR card, CSS card, Digital Radiohead and Victory Audio Adapter), Test, Integration and Evaluation,
 and Program Management Support.

Remarks

D. Acquisition Strategy
The CMOSS Mounted Form Factor (CMFF) program responds to Army requirements contained in the CMFF Abbreviated Capability Development Document (A-CDD),
approved on 4 January 2021. A new start in FY 2025, the program is approved as a Middle Tier of Acquisition (MTA) Rapid Prototyping (RP) as of 04 December 2025.
The MTA RP effort spans the initial five years of the program and will prototype the tactical radio communication components to include: Software Defined Radio (SDR)
cards, Cryptographic Subsystem (CSS) cards, Digital Radioheads (DRH) and Victory Audio Adapters (VAX). Multiple Other Transaction Authority (OTA) contracts will
be awarded to support the development prototype hardware and software solutions in support of this program. Market research has indicated sufficient interest and
availability to support competition across all communication components.

 The capabilities being developed within the CMFF Tactical Radios program represent the critical linchpin to the CMFF solution; convergence of legacy mounted radios
 into a single form factor is only achievable through execution of PM TR's CMFF Radio Card program. The capabilities developed under this MTA will be used to evaluate
 the technical feasibility of a modular system for ground and aviation platforms in support of the Army's C5ISR/EW CMFF. The prototypes will interoperate together within
 the CMFF MCI Chassis.

APPLICATIONS

Note
Program Element (PE) 0605331A / C2 Applications is established in Fiscal Year (FY) 2027 as part of a new funding structure for the NGC2 investment in modernization
of Army C2 systems. This project represents a funding line consolidation and structure change for the NGC2 Application Layer and does not constitute a new start
program or effort. Funding for NGC2 Application Layer activities was provided in FY 2026 under PE 0604541A Project BT3 / Common Operating Environment (COE),
PE 0604818A Project EK9 / Tactical Network Operations and Management, and PE 0604818A Project DL8 / Predictive Logistics, which are realigned under this PE
beginning in FY 2027.

A. Mission Description and Budget Item Justification
Army's Next Generation Command and Control (NGC2) is the Army's highest Command and Control (C2) modernization priority and will enhance the ability to conduct
multi-domain operations by integrating advanced technologies, improving interoperability, and increasing the speed and accuracy of decision-making processes. This
funding line supports the Command and Control (C2) Application Layer within the NGC2 ecosystem.

 A fully integrated C2 Application Layer is essential for maintaining operational superiority in a complex battlefield environment. The Army is modernizing its C2 approach
 to achieve battlefield superiority by implementing a horizontally integrated software services framework within the NGC2 ecosystem. The C2 Application Layer of
 this framework is dedicated to developing and delivering innovative common and warfighting applications that enhance synchronization and effectiveness across all
 warfighting functions (WfF) (Command and Control, Movement and Maneuver, Intelligence, Fires, Protection, and Sustainment) providing Commanders with globally
 accessible C2 Applications facilitating faster and better-informed decision making to outpace the enemy. Warfighters interact with the C2 Data Layer through the C2
 Application Layer using Common and Warfighter C2 Applications compatible with diverse form factors (i.e., tablets, radios, goggles) to enhance operational mobility. The
 C2 Application Layer leverages a flexible, open architecture and continuous integration and continuous delivery (CI/CD) pipelines for rapid integration and deployment
 of critical capabilities, supported by a modular C2 Data Layer. C2 Applications will rely on modern approaches, including CI/CD pipelines, application programming
 interfaces (APIs), software development kits (SDKs), Modular Open Systems Approach (MOSA), and Natural Language Processing (NLP).

 Project C2C / COMMON C2 APPLICATIONS delivers a suite of common applications (i.e., Clock, Map, Weather and Compass) and a globally accessible storefront for
 the C2 Application Layer of the NGC2 ecosystem, drawing from and contributing to the single, unified C2 Data Layer. This initiative ensures every soldier accesses the
 same consistent, real-time information, which feeds directly into essential Warfighter C2 Applications. The result is enhanced interoperability and a reliable operational
 picture for commanders, enabling faster and more effective decision-making. To ensure universal access, the storefront allows soldiers to find and download approved


PE 0605331A: C2 Applications                                                UNCLASSIFIED
                                                                                                                                                                   Volume 3d - 159
Army                                                                             Page 1 of 14                         R-1 Line #163

===== PAGE 192 =====
                                                                           UNCLASSIFIED
Exhibit R-2, RDT&E Budget Item Justification: PB 2027 Army                                                                               Date: April 2026
Appropriation/Budget Activity                                                    R-1 Program Element (Number/Name)
2040: Research, Development, Test & Evaluation, Army / BA 5: System              PE 0605331A / C2 Applications
Development & Demonstration (SDD)
 Common and Warfighter C2 Applications to their operational devices, much like a commercial app store. By employing a CI/CD pipeline, the project rapidly develops and
 integrates these tools, replacing numerous, disparate systems into a suite of common applications.

 Project C2D / WARFIGHTER C2 APPLICATIONS delivers a new generation of agile applications focused on enhancing synchronization and effectiveness across the
 six core warfighting functions for the C2 Application Layer of the NGC2 ecosystem. These specialized tools are designed to seamlessly interoperate with Common C2
 Applications, drawing from and contributing to the single, unified C2 Data Layer. Built to be inherently modular, scalable, and secure, Warfighter C2 Applications break
 down traditional warfighting silos and ensure operational resilience in Denied, Degraded, Intermittent, or Limited (DDIL) environments. By leveraging a CI/CD pipeline,
 Project C2D dramatically accelerates the deployment of Warfighter C2 capabilities from prototype to the warfighter. The result is a cohesive operational environment that
 provides commanders with shared situational awareness, enabling the swift, informed decisions necessary to achieve and maintain battlefield superiority.

 Funding has been realigned into this line to support the Army's Next Generation Command and Control (NGC2) initiative.

Quantity of RDT&E Articles                  -         -          -          -           -            -          -            -          -            -

A. Mission Description and Budget Item Justification
This funding supports the Army's Next Generation Command and Control (NGC2) initiative to modernize the Command and Control (C2) systems, Applications Layer.

 Project C2C / COMMON C2 APPLICATIONS is established in Fiscal Year (FY) 2027 as part of a new funding structure for the NGC2 investment in modernization of
 Army C2 systems. This project represents a funding line consolidation and structure change for the NGC2 Application Layer and does not constitute a new start program
 or effort. Funding for NGC2 Application Layer activities was provided in FY 2026 under PE 0604541A Project BT3 / Common Operating Environment (COE), which is
 realigned under this PE beginning in FY 2027.

 COMMON C2 APPLICATIONS delivers a suite of common applications (i.e., Clock, Map, Weather and Compass) and a globally accessible storefront for the C2
 Application Layer of the NGC2 ecosystem, drawing from and contributing to the single, unified C2 Data Layer. This initiative ensures every soldier accesses the
 same consistent, real-time information, which feeds directly into essential warfighting applications. The result is enhanced interoperability and a reliable operational
 picture for commanders, enabling faster and more effective decision-making. To ensure universal access, the storefront allows soldiers to find and download approved
 Common and Warfighter C2 Applications to their operational devices, much like a commercial app store. By employing a CI/CD pipeline, the project rapidly develops and
 integrates these tools, replacing numerous, disparate systems into a suite of common applications.

 FY 2027 funding supports the customization, prototyping, integration, and deployment of Common C2 Applications and a globally accessible storefront into the C2
 Application Layer of the NGC2 ecosystem. It includes a robust CI/CD pipeline, testing and evaluation, and training.

B. Accomplishments/Planned Programs ($ in Millions)                                                                                    FY 2025           FY 2026        FY 2027
Title: C2C Continuous Integration/Continuous Delivery (CI/CD)                                                                                 -                 -           4.032
Description: C2C Continuous Integration/Continuous Delivery (CI/CD) funding will establish a robust CI/CD pipeline that will
facilitate the rapid prototyping, customization, integration, testing, and deployment of Common C2 Applications into the C2
Application Layer of the NGC2 ecosystem. The CI/CD approach will leverage multiple product teams to build a modernized C2
Application Layer. CI/CD practices accelerate the deployment of updates and new features with minimal disruption, fostering a
culture of rapid adaptation based on user feedback and operational needs. The effort streamlines the integration of existing Army
systems into a flexible, open architecture, enhancing operational effectiveness and collaboration across all teams, providing the
digital foundation to get vital capabilities to the warfighter faster.

FY 2027 Plans:

D. Acquisition Strategy
The Next Generation Command and Control (NGC2) Software Acquisition Pathway (SWP) program continued in the Planning Phase during FY26. The program consists
of Data and Applications Layer efforts to define, develop, test and refine software solutions that support the overall NGC2 capability. Experimentation with 4th and 25th
Infantry Divisions, using industry-leading solutions, will provide insights to inform a transition into two Execution Phase SWP programs, one for the Data Layer and one
for the Applications Layer to deliver NGC2 data and applications capabilities to the Warfighter.

 Applications Layer efforts focus on leveraging existing capabilities paired with modernization and industry solutions, while engaging multiple teams to integrate
 innovative capabilities, with a key demonstration planned for PCC6 in Fiscal Year 2026. The Applications layer will deliver a suite of common and warfighting-function-
 specific applications via a globally accessible application storefront. Mission command applications will streamline planning, execution, and collaboration across all
 warfighting functions to enable commanders to achieve more effective and timely decision-making. The Applications Layer SWP Execution Phase program will execute
 via a Continuous Integration/ Continuous Deployment (CI/CD) construct to iteratively update and deploy applications beginning in FY27.

A. Mission Description and Budget Item Justification
This funding supports the Army's Next Generation Command and Control (NGC2) initiative to modernize the Command and Control (C2) systems, Application Layer.

 Project C2D / WARFIGHTER C2 APPLICATIONS is established in Fiscal Year (FY) 2027 as part of a new funding structure for the NGC2 investment in modernization of
 Army C2 systems. This project represents a funding line consolidation and structure change for the NGC2 Application Layer and does not constitute a new start program
 or effort. Funding for NGC2 Application Layer activities was provided in FY 2026 under PE 0604818A Project EK9 / Tactical Network Operations and Management, and
 PE 0604818A Project DL8 / Predictive Logistics, which are realigned under this PE beginning in FY 2027.

 WARFIGHTER C2 APPLICATIONS delivers a new generation of agile applications focused on enhancing synchronization and effectiveness across the six core
 warfighting functions (WfF) (Command and Control, Movement and Maneuver, Intelligence, Fires, Protection, and Sustainment) for the C2 Application Layer of the
 NGC2 ecosystem. These specialized tools are designed to seamlessly interoperate with Common C2 Applications, drawing from and contributing to the single, unified
 C2 Data Layer. Built to be inherently modular, scalable, and secure, Warfighter C2 Applications break down traditional warfighting silos and ensure operational resilience
 in Denied, Degraded, Intermittent, or Limited (DDIL) environments. By leveraging a CI/CD pipeline, Project C2D dramatically accelerates the deployment of Warfighter
 C2 capabilities from prototype to the warfighter. The result is a cohesive operational environment that provides commanders with shared situational awareness, enabling
 the swift, informed decisions necessary to achieve and maintain battlefield superiority.

 FY 2027 funding supports the customization, prototyping, integration, and deployment of Warfighting C2 Applications into the C2 Application Layer of the NGC2
 ecosystem. It includes a robust CI/CD pipeline, testing and evaluation, and training.

B. Accomplishments/Planned Programs ($ in Millions)                                                                                       FY 2025           FY 2026        FY 2027

facilitates rapid prototyping, customization, integration, testing, and deployment of Warfighting C2 Applications into the C2
Application Layer of the NGC2 ecosystem. The CI/CD approach will leverage multiple product teams to build a modernized C2
Application Layer. CI/CD practices accelerate the deployment of updates and new features with minimal disruption, fostering a
culture of rapid adaptation based on user feedback and operational needs. The effort streamlines the integration of existing Army
systems into a flexible, open architecture, enhancing operational effectiveness and collaboration across all teams, providing the
digital foundation to get vital capabilities to the warfighter faster.

C2F: ARTIFICIAL                            -         -           -      58.503          -       58.503     78.015      110.204       91.037       89.216         0.000    426.975
INTELLIGENCE/MACHINE
LEARNING

Note
Program Element (PE) 060533A C2 Data is established in FY 2027 as part of a new funding structure for the Next Generation Command and Control (NGC2)
investment to modernize Army Command and Control (C2) systems. A funding line consolidation and structure change for the NGC2 investment provides clarity and
transparency for the NGC2 four-layered approach (Data, Applications, Infrastructure and Transport). This PE supports NGC2 efforts under the Data Layer (which
consists of C2 Data (Project C2E) and Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning (AI/ML) (Project C2F)) and does not constitute a new program or effort. Funding for
NGC2 Data Layer activities was provided in FY 2026 under PE 0604541/BT5 (Integrated Tactical Network/Enterprise Network) and PE 06054818/EJ6 (Tactical
Enhancements), which are realigned under this PE beginning in FY 2027.

A. Mission Description and Budget Item Justification
NGC2 is the Army's highest Command and Control (C2) modernization priority and will enhance the ability to conduct multi-domain operations by integrating advanced
technologies, improving interoperability, and increasing the speed and accuracy of decision-making processes. This funding line supports the C2 Data Layer within the
NGC2 ecosystem providing commanders a common layer of persistently available C2 data facilitating faster and better-informed decision making to outpace the enemy.
A fully integrated C2 Data Layer using real-time data across warfighting functions provides seamless Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning (AI/ML) for soldiers and
commanders facilitating rapid Sensor-to-Shooter targeting, fires, airspace management, cyber, and non-kinetic effects capabilities. Inclusive of common services for
credentialing, data ingestion and data management that enable applications.

C2E/C2 Data: The C2 Data layer delivers an integrated, seamless, threat informed, scalable, resilient, converged and meshed data platform inclusive of common
services for credentialing, data ingestion and data management with sufficient capacity at each echelon, enabling dynamic and collective Warfighting Command and
Control while operating in a disrupted, disconnected, intermittent and low-bandwidth (DDIL) tactical environment.

C2F/AI/ML: The C2 Data layer AI/ML decision tools augment operations at the speed and scale required by rapidly synthesizing large volumes of data giving
commanders the right information, at the right time, to support the right decision. The C2 Data layer AI/ML tools enable consistent and constant evolution of the NGC2
ecosystem maintaining pace with the accelerated change in the modern digital battlefield.

FY 2027 funding supports contract award deploying the C2 Data layer system to the Army, and ensuring continuous integration and continuous deployment (CI/CD) for
a secure, high performing, and adaptive centerpiece to the NGC2 technology stack. FY 2027 funding additionally supports integration of AI/ML decision tools into the C2
data layer system optimizing the ecosystem and providing commanders a digital data-centric tactical advantage. This funding encompasses the continuation of network
