Note
Program Element (PE) 060533A C2 Data is established in FY 2027 as part of a new funding structure for the Next Generation Command and Control (NGC2)
investment to modernize Army Command and Control (C2) systems. A funding line consolidation and structure change for the NGC2 investment provides clarity and
transparency for the NGC2 four-layered approach (Data, Applications, Infrastructure and Transport). This PE supports NGC2 efforts under the Data Layer (which
consists of C2 Data (Project C2E) and does not constitute a new program or effort. Funding for NGC2 Data Layer activities was provided in FY 2026 under PE 654818/
EJ6 (Tactical Enhancements), which is realigned under this PE beginning in FY 2027.

A. Mission Description and Budget Item Justification
Project C2E / C2 Data is established in Fiscal Year (FY) 2027 as part of a new funding structure for the NGC2 investment in modernization of Army C2 systems. This
project represents a funding line consolidation and structure change for the NGC2 Data Layer and does not constitute a new start program or effort. Funding for NGC2
Data Layer activities was provided in FY 2026 under PE 0604818A Project EJ6 / Tactical Enhancement, which is realigned under this PE beginning in FY 2027.

The Army's data layer provides a unified environment that ingests, transforms, and normalizes data from disparate systems to ensure it is trusted, usable, and
interoperable across missions and applications. It delivers secure credentialing and identity-based access controls so users, systems, and automated agents can
interact with information according to policy and classification. The layer stores, organizes, and manages data at enterprise scale while applying metadata tagging
to enable rapid discovery, retrieval, and fusion. It exposes common data services-such as translation, validation, cataloging, and orchestration-that standardize how
information moves across networks and platforms. Together, these functions form the foundation for advanced analytics, AI/ML, and decision-support tools that improve
the speed and quality of operations.

The Army's data layer includes the operating system and common core services (including interface applications, analytics, network health and cybersecurity, training
and simulation). Core services are key to enabling applications in a data-centric network with increased efficiency in overall data/network management. Core services
will leverage industry standard protocols for identity authentication and access control at the point of need in denied, disrupted, intermittent and limited environments and
enable the tactical environment to meet Department of War and Army Zero Trust requirements.

FY 2027 funding supports contract awards deploying C2 Data layer system to the Army, and ensuring continuous integration and continuous deployment (CI/CD) for a
secure, high performing, and adaptive centerpiece to the NGC2 technology stack. This funding encompasses the continuation of network operation and credentialing
management tools development with additional capabilities for data ingestion, and intelligent data orchestration enabling commanders to dynamically reconfigure or
reallocate resources to match emergent mission needs against a thinking, data-centric adversary.

B. Accomplishments/Planned Programs ($ in Millions)                                                                                        FY 2025           FY 2026        FY 2027
Title: C2 Data Layer Continuous Integration and Continuous Deployment (CI/CD)                                                                     -                 -         220.275

PE 0605332A: C2 Data                                                        UNCLASSIFIED
                                                                                                                                                                    Volume 3d - 175
Army                                                                              Page 3 of 15                         R-1 Line #164

Appropriation/Budget Activity                                                      R-1 Program Element (Number/Name)                  Project (Number/Name)
2040 / 5                                                                           PE 0605332A / C2 Data                              C2E / C2 DATA

B. Accomplishments/Planned Programs ($ in Millions)                                                                                         FY 2025     FY 2026         FY 2027
Description: The Army's data layer leverages a data-centric CI/CD approach, utilizing a Modular Open Systems Architecture
(MOSA) and open APIs to integrate evolving vendor solutions seamlessly. This strategy facilitates continuous iteration and rapid
execution of new data layer capabilities, moving away from rigid systems to a flexible, software-driven model that can adapt
quickly to mission needs. By employing a competitive vendor ecosystem and prototype events, the Army ensures the rapid,
continuous development and refinement of its data layer in live operational conditions. This overall transformation in acquisition
and development uses CI/CD to push a secure, adaptive, and global common data picture to the tactical edge, ultimately
achieving "decision dominance" on the battlefield.

Continuous Integration ensures the latest technologies are routinely and cyclically developed and incorporated in System
Integration Laboratories (SILs) and provided to soldiers in brigade and divisional exercises. The continuous integration and
validation allow for rapid updating, cybersecurity compliance, risk management, integration, field event testing, logistical support,
configuration control and appropriate software materiel release.

Continuous Delivery and deployment is an extension of Continuous Integration that provides a pipeline of the most current
capabilities to the warfighter ensuring the best and most relevant capability to provide enemy overmatch and battlefield superiority.
It automates the release of the successfully tested and validated code to a production-like environment and includes the
deployment, training, feedback, across every fielded unit. With Continuous Delivery, the software is always in a deployable state,
ready to be released to the warfighter.

FY 2027 Plans:
FY 27 funds CI/CD of the Data capability. It will mature CI/CD processes to integrate emerging technology and scaled capability
via algorithms, sensor data, legacy capabilities into the data layer baseline and award contract(s) to deploy the Data Layer
capability across the Army. Additionally, it will continue analysis of commercial capabilities, evaluating their suitability and
potential for inclusion in the NGC2 Data layer and compatibility with the NGC2 ecosystem. Team lead vendors will integrate viable
capabilities into the data layer, ensuring the latest technology is available for use by Warfighters.
FY 2026 to FY 2027 Increase/Decrease Statement:
FY 2027 increase due to project established under a new funding structure to support NGC2 Data Layer efforts. Increase from
prior funded year due to integration/deployment of additional data layer capabilities across the Army and the intent to accelerate
deployment timelines.
Title: C2 Data Layer Test/Evaluation/Validation/Verification (TEVV)                                                                                -               -        8.159
Description: The test strategy will focus on continuous verification and validation within both developmental and operational
environments to assess Safety, Suitability, Supportability, Survivability and Sustainability. This will include iterative technical
evaluations including Soldier Touch Points (STPs), and continuous embedded integration with users.

D. Acquisition Strategy
The Software Acquisition Pathway (SWP) program, currently in the Planning Phase (PP), supports the Next Generation Command and Control (NGC2) Data and
Applications Layers established to satisfy requirements captured in the NGC2 Capability Characteristics of Need (CoN), dated 21 May 2024, NGC2 Initial Capability
Document (ICD), dated 23 September 2025, and NGC2 CoN Update 2 Annex for Data Layer, dated 23 September 2025. This funding line supports the C2 Data
Layer, which provides commanders persistently available C2 data to facilitate faster and better-informed decisions that outpace adversaries. A fully integrated C2 Data
Layer using real-time data across warfighting functions provides soldiers and commanders seamless Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning and common services

 for credentialing, data ingestion and data management that enable applications. Adoption of a Continuous Integration/Continuous Deployment (CI/CD) construct to
 iteratively update and deploy data layer and common services capabilities will enable the NGC2 investment in modernizing the Army's C2 data layer system.

The NGC2 prototypes Other Transaction Authority (OTA) contract, awarded on 17 July 2025, and the Commercial Solutions Opening (CSO)/OTA contract, awarded
on 28 August 2025, will deliver prototype architectures for NGC2 data layer capabilities to 4ID and 25ID in accordance with the approved NGC2 ICD. These contracts
implemented a Team Lead approach whereby the lead vendor evaluates and on-/off-boards capabilities that are incorporated into or withdrawn from the integrated
solutions that provide the AI/ML-enhanced C2 Data layer. Additional opportunities to potentially engage industry further exist through ongoing Small Business Innovation
Research projects and Science & Technology activities that could augment the CI/CD pipeline or lead to the creation of new CI/CD pipelines.

The schedule (R-4) reflects a strategy of CI/CD leveraging iterative development and deployment of modernized capability using automated software engineering tools.

Current strategy encompasses the continuation of network operation and credentialing management tools development with additional capabilities for data ingestion,
and intelligent data orchestration enabling commanders to dynamically reconfigure or reallocate resources to match emergent mission needs against a thinking, data-
centric adversary. The data layer and common services will continue to leverage industry's best practices for Development, Security, and Operations (DevSecOps)
throughout the life of the program.

Note
Program Element (PE) 060533A C2 Data is established in FY 2027 as part of a new funding structure for the Next Generation Command and Control (NGC2)
investment to modernize Army Command and Control (C2) systems. A funding line consolidation and structure change for the NGC2 investment provides clarity and
transparency for the NGC2 four-layered approach (Data, Applications, Infrastructure and Transport). This PE supports NGC2 efforts under the Data Layer (Artificial
Intelligence/Machine Learning (AI/ML) (Project C2F)) and does not constitute a new program or effort. Funding for NGC2 Data Layer activities was provided in FY 2026
under PE 0604818/EJ6 (Tactical Enhancements), which is realigned under this PE beginning in FY 2027.

A. Mission Description and Budget Item Justification
Project C2F / Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning is established in Fiscal Year (FY) 2027 as part of a new funding structure for the NGC2 investment in modernization
of Army C2 systems. This project represents a funding line consolidation and structure change for the NGC2 Data Layer and does not constitute a new start program
or effort. Funding for NGC2 Data Layer activities was provided in FY 2026 under PE 0604818A Project EJ6 / Tactical Enhancement, which is realigned under this PE
beginning in FY 2027.

The C2 Data layer Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning (AI/ML) decision tools augment operations at the speed and scale required by rapidly synthesizing large
volumes of data giving commanders the right information, at the right time, to support the right decision. The C2 Data layer AI/ML tools enable consistent and constant
evolution of the NGC2 ecosystem maintaining pace with the accelerated change in the modern digital battlefield. Recognizing patterns and the context behind the data,
AI/ML tools reveal connections that might otherwise go unnoticed, leading to missed opportunities that could make the difference between success or failure. The AI/
ML toolbox will be enhanced by the inclusion of AI Operations and Services (AIOPS) capabilities that began as multiple Small Business Innovation Research efforts and
were realigned to the C2 Data layer as part of the NGC2 structure changes.

FY 2027 funding supports the development and integration of AI/ML decision tools in the C2 data layer system optimizing the NGC2 ecosystem.

B. Accomplishments/Planned Programs ($ in Millions)                                                                                          FY 2025           FY 2026        FY 2027

Description: AI/ML decision tools to support the NGC2 Data Layer and the larger NGC2 ecosystem, with emphasis on the ability
to integrate with the data layer Continuous Integration/Continuous Deployment (CI/CD) process. Robust use cases are essential
to support the development of effective AI/ML capabilities, which requires coordination with representative Army units. Use cases

D. Acquisition Strategy
The Software Acquisition Pathway (SWP) program, currently in the Planning Phase (PP), supports the Next Generation Command and Control (NGC2) Data and
Applications Layers established to satisfy requirements captured in the NGC2 Capability Characteristics of Need (CoN), dated 21 May 2024, NGC2 Initial Capability
Document (ICD), dated 23 September 2025, and NGC2 CoN Update 2 Annex for Data Layer, dated 23 September 2025. This funding line supports the integration of
Artificial Intelligence / Machine Learning (AI/ML) tools into the C2 Data Layer to augment command efforts by analyzing copious amounts of data from various sources
and synthesizing it to inform better decisions at the speed of relevance given the dynamics anticipated in a digital battlefield. Adoption of a Continuous Integration/
Continuous Deployment (CI/CD) construct to iteratively update and deploy AI/ML tools will enable the NGC2 investment in modernizing the Army's C2 data layer system.

The NGC2 prototypes Other Transaction Authority (OTA) contract, awarded on 17 July 2025, and the Commercial Solutions Opening (CSO)/OTA contract, awarded on
28 August 2025, will deliver prototype architectures for NGC2 data layer capabilities to 4ID and 25ID in accordance with the approved NGC2 ICD. The schedule (R-4)
reflects a strategy of CI/CD leveraging iterative development and deployment of modernized AI/ML enabling tools using automated software engineering tools.

Current strategy encompasses the continuation of development and integration for AI/ML tools to augment operations at the speed and scale required to synthesize
large volumes of data and give commanders the right information, at the right time, to support the right decision. The AI/ML toolbox will be enhanced by the inclusion of
AI Operations and Services (AIOPS) capabilities that began as multiple Small Business Innovation Research efforts and were realigned to the C2 Data layer as part of
the NGC2 structure changes.

DEVELOPMENT

A. Mission Description and Budget Item Justification
Program Element (PE) 0605333A Command and Control (C2) Infrastructure is established in FY 2027 as part of a new funding structure for the Next Generation
Command and Control (NGC2) investment in modernization of Army C2 systems. A funding line consolidation and structure change for the NGC2 investment provides
clarity and transparency for the NGC2 four-layered approach (Applications, Data, Transport, and Infrastructure).

 This C2 Infrastructure PE supports NGC2 efforts under the Infrastructure layer and does not constitute a new program or effort. Funding for NGC2 Infrastructure
 activities was provided in FY2026 under PE 0604818A Project 323 Common Hardware Systems, PE 0608418A Project C34 Army Tactical Command and Control
 Systems Engineering, PE 0604818A Project ER9 Expeditionary Army Command Post, PE 0604805A Project DH5 Mounted Form Factor Chassis , PE 0605236A Project
 CQ1 Tactical Communication Network Evaluation and PE 0604541A Project BT2 Command Post Mobility/Survivability.

 Project C2G, Modular C2 Infrastructure supports the Command, Control, Communications, Computers, Cyber, Intelligence, Surveillance and Reconnaissance (C5ISR)
 Modular Open Suite of Standards (CMOSS) Mounted Form Factor (CMFF), a defined suite of open architecture which utilizes a modular open systems approach
 (MOSA) that implements C5ISR capabilities away from separate "boxes" on individual platforms and ensures commonality across multiple vehicles and platforms. The
 CMFF Mounted Common Infrastructure (MCI) Chassis will use open standards which make it simpler and more cost-effective to upgrade capabilities and/or keep pace
 with commercial technology by eliminating complex integration challenges and proprietary interfaces.

 Project C2H, C2 SYSTEMS ENGINEERING supports the Army's Network Modernization Strategy priorities including Army Unified Network Plan, Multi-Domain
 Operations, Joint All Domain Command and Control (JADC2), Data Modernization and emerging data-centric requirements. This funding supports the modernization of
 C2 systems by integrating complex networked systems at scale. It enables the continuous design, testing, and validation of multiple network architectures, incorporating
 direct end-user feedback to enhance the security and resiliency and ensure network cohesiveness and effectiveness. This project coordinates technical efforts across
 and outside of CPE Command, Control, Communications, and Network (C3N) to ensure integration with the current and future Mission Command Network; provides
 technical support to programs, informing design and solutions with specific emphasis on the ability for different program efforts to be integrated and interoperable with
 one another.

2040: Research, Development, Test & Evaluation, Army / BA 5: System            PE 0605333A / C2 Infrastructure
Development & Demonstration (SDD)
 Project C2I, Mounted C2 Development, supports Army's initiative to modernize C2 systems. Mounted C2 Development delivers a drop-in replacement for existing
 systems, using available platform space and power applications. The capability provides on-the-move bandwidth needed for Warfighting Applications in support of the
 Army NGC2 initiative to modernize C2 systems, Infrastructure layer. A modular open systems approach is used to maximize flexibility through rapid technology insertion
 and non-proprietary technologies and provides resilient communications. Mounted C2 Development focuses on continuous improvements for on the move operations,
 modular command posts, and survivability.

 Project C2G, Modular C2 Infrastructure: FY 2027 funding request completes the CMFF MCI Block 1 chassis product development effort (hardware and software
 development and prototype manufacturing), conduct the Block 1 Soldier Touch Point (STP) Development Test, and supports the start of Block 2 chassis development
 efforts. The total cost of the CMFF MCI Middle Tier of Acquisition (MTA) Rapid Prototyping (RP) effort is $82.5 million RDT&E from FY25 to FY27. The remainder of the
 CMFF MCI MTA RP is fully funded across the Future Years Defense Program

 Project C2H, C2 SYSTEMS ENGINEERING: FY 2027 funding request supports the CPE C3N System of Systems (SoS) Engineering and integration, acquisition
 management, testing, fielding and sustainment support to ensure interoperability and affordability within the CPE C3N portfolio, with increased emphasis on immediate
 Warfighter needs as well as leveraging emerging technologies. This funding supports the modernization of C2 systems by integrating complex networked systems at
 scale. It enables the continuous design, testing, and validation of multiple network architectures, incorporating direct end-user feedback to enhance the security and
 resiliency and ensure network cohesiveness and effectiveness.

 Project C2I, Mounted C2 Development: FY 2027 funding request supports development and systems engineering efforts, as well as enhancing command post
 capabilities. This funding emphasizes continuous improvements for on-the-move operations, modular command posts, and survivability. Additionally, it covers test and
 evaluation activities, logistical support, and program management to ensure the successful implementation of these initiatives.

 Funding has been realigned into this line to support the Army's Next Generation Command and Control (NGC2) initiative.

A. Mission Description and Budget Item Justification
Project C2G: Modular Command and Control (C2) Infrastructure is established in FY 2027 as part of a new funding structure for the Next Generation Command
and Control (NGC2) investment in the modernization of Army C2 systems. This project represents a funding line consolidation and structure change for the NGC2
Infrastructure Layer and does not constitute a new program or effort.

 Funding for NGC2 Infrastructure activities was provided in FY2026 under PE 0604818A Project 323 Common Hardware Systems and PE 0604805A Project DH5
 Mounted Form Factor Chassis.

 Command, Control, Communications, Computers, Cyber, Intelligence, Surveillance and Reconnaissance (C5ISR) Modular Open Suite of Standards (CMOSS) Mounted
 Form Factor (CMFF) is a defined suite of open architecture which utilizes a modular open systems approach (MOSA) that implements C5ISR capabilities away from
 separate "boxes" on individual platforms and ensures commonality across multiple vehicles and platforms. The CMFF Mounted Common Infrastructure (MCI) Chassis
 will use open standards which make it simpler and more cost-effective to upgrade capabilities and/or keep pace with commercial technology by eliminating complex
 integration challenges and proprietary interfaces.

 The CMFF capability can only be realized when paired with the development of associated capability cards for integration into the chassis and peripheral enabling
 devices, such as antennae and appropriate user interfaces. Other programs are responsible for funding and developing the capability cards and peripheral devices;
 the CMFF MCI Chassis program is responsible for chassis development, system of system integration of capability cards and external resources into the chassis and
 platform integration.

 The total cost of the CMFF MCI Middle Tier of Acquisition (MTA) Rapid Prototyping (RP) effort is $82.5 million RDT&E from FY25 to FY27. The remainder of the CMFF
 MCI MTA RP is fully funded across the Future Years Defense Program.

 FY 2027 funding will complete CMFF MCI Block 1 chassis product development effort (hardware and software development and prototype manufacturing), conduct the
 Block 1 Soldier Touch Point (STP) Development Test, and supports the start of Block 2 chassis development efforts.

C. Other Program Funding Summary ($ in Millions)
Remarks

D. Acquisition Strategy
The CMOSS Mounted Form Factor (CMFF) Mounted Common Infrastructure (MCI) Chassis program responds to Army requirements in the approved CMFF
Abbreviated Capability Development Document (A-CDD), validated on 4 January 2021, and is pursuing a Middle Tier of Acquisition (MTA) Rapid Prototyping (RP)
acquisition pathway, which was initiated in FY2025. The MTA RP effort spans five years and will prototype the chassis and software that manages the operation of the
chassis, as well as integrate the capability cards and peripheral enabling devices, assuring the integrated solution operates as required. (The capability cards deliver
communications, situational awareness, and positioning, navigating, and timing (PNT) capabilities that will be provided by external programs).

 The project will converge the capability cards into a single CMOSS compliant form factor (chassis) mounted on selected military ground and aviation platforms that can
 be demonstrated in a relevant operational environment. The control software in the chassis will enable simultaneous operations of the CMFF MCI and capability cards
 without interference between the capability cards and the systems mounted on the platforms, utilizing open standard/vendor agnostic interfaces presented via a common
 user-interface (UI). This will meet current systems performance parameters and position the program to integrate capability cards that host legacy ("stove-piped") and
 emerging capabilities.

 The MTA RP program will execute hardware and software prototype development of the chassis for ground and aviation variants in two blocks, focusing initially
 on light tracked and wheeled vehicles and early aviation prototyping (Block 1) and then the Combat platforms and initial Future Vertical Lift System Integration Lab
 implementation and firing platforms (Block 2).

 Market research indicates sufficient interest and availability to support competition for CMFF MCI. Other Transaction Authorities will be utilized to prototype the chassis
 and common user interface system control software.

A. Mission Description and Budget Item Justification
Project C2H: Command and Control (C2) SYSTEMS ENGINEERING is established in FY 2027 as part of a new funding structure for the Next Generation Command and
Control (NGC2) investment in modernization of Army C2 systems. This project represents a funding line consolidation and structure change for the NGC2 Infrastructure
Layer and does not constitute a new program or effort.

 Funding for NGC2 Infrastructure activities was provided in FY2026 under PE 0604818A Project C34 Army Tactical Command and Control Systems Engineering and PE
 0604541A Project BT2 Command Post Mobility/Survivability.

 NGC2 is one of the Army's highest modernization priorities and will enhance the ability to conduct multi-domain operations by integrating advanced technologies,
 improving interoperability, and increasing the speed and accuracy of decision-making processes.

 This line specifically supports Project C2 SYSTEMS ENGINEERING, which supports the Army's Network Modernization Strategy priorities including Army Unified
 Network Plan, Multi-Domain Operations, Joint All Domain Command and Control (JADC2), Data Modernization and emerging data-centric requirements. This project
 coordinates technical efforts across and outside of CPE Command, Control, Information Network (C2IN) to ensure integration with the current and future Mission
 Command Network; provides technical support to programs, informing design and solutions with specific emphasis on the ability for different program efforts to be
 integrated and interoperable with one another.

 FY2027 funding request supports the CPE C2IN System of Systems (SoS) Engineering and integration, acquisition management, testing, fielding and sustainment
 support to ensure interoperability and affordability within the CPE C2IN portfolio, with increased emphasis on immediate Warfighter needs as well as leveraging
 emerging technologies. This funding supports the modernization of C2 systems by integrating complex networked systems at scale. It enables the continuous design,
 testing, and validation of multiple network architectures, incorporating direct end-user feedback to enhance the security and resiliency and ensure network cohesiveness
 and effectiveness.

D. Acquisition Strategy

A. Mission Description and Budget Item Justification
Project C2I: Mounted Command and Control (C2) Development is established in FY 2027 as part of a new funding structure for the Next Generation Command and
Control (NGC2) investment in modernization of Army C2 systems. This project represents a funding line consolidation and structure change for the NGC2 Infrastructure
layer and does not constitute a new program or effort.

 Funding for NGC2 Infrastructure activities was provided in FY2026 under PE 0604818A Project ER9 Expeditionary Army Command Post and PE 0605236A Project CQ1
 Tactical Communication Network Evaluation.

 Command posts provide locations from which commanders, assisted by their staffs, command operations and integrate/synchronize combat power to accomplish
 missions across the range of military operations. Commanders organize mission command systems into command posts based on mission requirements and the
 situation that will best assist them in exercising command and control of assigned and attached forces. Command post survivability is vital to mission success.

 Depending on the threat, command posts need to remain small and highly mobile, especially at echelons below Division. Command posts are easily acquired and
 targeted by our adversaries when concentrated. Command post survivability is increased via dispersion, reduced size, redundancy, mobility, as well as camouflage
 and concealment. Command post survivability is further enhanced via dismounted operations that can take advantage of cover or shielding by terrain features or urban
 structures.

 This project funds development to provide a drop-in replacement for existing systems, using available platform space and power applications. The capability provides
 on-the-move bandwidth needed for Warfighting Applications in support of the Army NGC2 initiative to modernize C2 systems, Infrastructure layer. A modular open
 systems approach is used to maximize flexibility through rapid technology insertion and non-proprietary technologies and provides resilient communications.

 FY2027 funding supports development and systems engineering efforts, as well as enhancing command post capabilities. This funding emphasizes continuous
 improvements for on-the-move operations, modular command posts, and survivability. Additionally, it covers test and evaluation activities, logistical support, and
 program management to ensure the successful implementation of these initiatives.

B. Accomplishments/Planned Programs ($ in Millions)                                                                                        FY 2025           FY 2026        FY 2027
Title: Product Development                                                                                                                        -                 -          16.286
Description: Product Development is dedicated to the comprehensive design, integration, fabrication, and prototyping of
command post platforms, specifically targeting capability gaps within Army Command Post formations and enhancing capabilities.

D. Acquisition Strategy
This funding line supports Army initiative to modernize Command and Control (C2) systems, Infrastructure layer.

 The Army's command posts are modernizing to be more flexible, iterative, and On-The Move (OTM). Commanders want modularity and the ability to change and adapt
 the command post based on their assigned mission set. Future Command Post improvements will incorporate a combination of emerging and existing innovations, such
 as enhanced on-the move and voice command functionalities. This approach will enable the Army to seamlessly update the capabilities of existing units without requiring
 equipment downtime, ensuring continuous operational readiness. The Army continues to develop innovative solutions to address mobile and flexible command post
 requirements.

 The C2 Fix Directed Requirement provides the authority to proceed with the development of capabilities to improve the command post's modularity, scalability, and
 mobility, enabling more flexible and adaptable operational configurations. Development efforts are focused on enhancing capabilities to establish a robust infrastructure
 supporting advanced networking, communications, and electronic signature management.

 The capability satisfies requirements in the NGC2 ICD and the Infrastructure and Transport Annexes. A modular open systems approach is used to maximize flexibility
 through rapid technology insertion and non-proprietary technologies and provides resilient communications. This is a drop-in replacement for existing systems, utilizing
 available platform space and power allocations.

Note
The FY 2026 spend plan amount for Unmanned Aerial Systems Launched Effects Systems Development includes $357,529 thousand of discretionary and $15,000
thousand of mandatory (reconciliation) for a total of $372,529 thousand.

A. Mission Description and Budget Item Justification
This capability portfolio provides support to the Army's UAS Priority to deliver cutting-edge technology and rapidly evolving critical capabilities.

 Project A56 - This effort was realigned from PE 0609345A/ Project A46 beginning FY 2027 and continues work previously performed under that PE. Long Range
 Precision Munition (LRPM) is the lethal variant of the Launched Effects (LE) Family of Systems which provide a range of capabilities through variations of platforms,
 payloads, and mission systems. LE provides tactical and operational flexibility through collaboration of heterogeneous groups of LE launched from multiple domains
 (land and air) in conjunction with other manned platforms, C2, unmanned systems, and sensors throughout the operational environment. The ability to interoperate
 and coordinate with other LE systems at long ranges and adapt to changing threats is a core concept of the Launched Effects Abbreviated Capability Development
 Document validated in June 2024. The primary target set for LRPM is Integrated Air Defense Systems. LRPM will provide Army Aviation and Ground Forces with a
 precise long range munition system to rapidly respond in a combat environment to improve the lethality and stand-off of Warfighters and aviation platforms in an Anti-
 Access Area Denial (A2AD) and positioning, navigation, and timing (PNT) denied environment. The total cost of the LRPM Middle Tier of Acquisition (MTA) Rapid
 Prototyping (RP) effort is $122.5 million RDT&E from FY24 to FY29. The LRPM MTA-RP is fully funded across the Future Years Defense Program.

PE 0605345A: Unmanned Aerial Systems Launched Effects...                      UNCLASSIFIED
                                                                                                                                                                     Volume 3d - 209
Army                                                                              Page 1 of 48                          R-1 Line #166

===== PAGE 242 =====
                                                                            UNCLASSIFIED
Exhibit R-2, RDT&E Budget Item Justification: PB 2027 Army                                                                                 Date: April 2026
Appropriation/Budget Activity                                                    R-1 Program Element (Number/Name)
2040: Research, Development, Test & Evaluation, Army / BA 5: System              PE 0605345A / Unmanned Aerial Systems Launched Effects Systems Development
Development & Demonstration (SDD)

 Project A58 - Lethal Semi-Autonomous Aerial Unmanned System - Engineering Development: Mobile Brigade Combat Teams (MBCTs) lack adequate organic
 capabilities at echelon to apply immediate, point, long range, and direct fire effects to destroy tanks, light armored vehicles, hardened targets, targets in defilade, and
 personnel targets, while producing minimal collateral damage in complex terrain in all environmental conditions. Development will focus on the Fire Control Stations
 (FCS), All-Up Round (AUR), and System Architecture. System Architecture will include integrating with Soldier Borne Mission command (SBMC), Next Generation
 Command and Control (NGC2) and complete Universal Vehicle Control (UVC) compliance and integration. Develop technology for incorporation into the CDD update,
 for the enduring program, and conduct tech enhancements from current global conflicts. The funding contained within this budget line and project will be used to conduct
 evaluations of technologies and capabilities in the loitering munitions capability space, hardware and system architecture engineering and integration activities, and
 testing and evaluation activities. In addition, LASSO is supporting Transformation in Contact (TiC) in support of Indo-Pacific efforts to expand prepositioned stocks and
 increase deployments and exercises to support strategic presence in the region. The total cost of the Low Altitude Stalking and Strike Ordinance (LASSO) Middle Tier of
 Acquisition (MTA) effort is $209.4 million RDT&E from FY25 to FY29.

 Project A59 - Soldier Borne Sensor (SBS): The SBS is a small unmanned aerial vehicle. The SBS provides a near term solution to three Army War-fighting Challenges
 at the Infantry Squad level: develop situational understanding, conduct air-ground reconnaissance, and conduct joint combined arms maneuver. The system is simple
 to deploy and use to support the squad leader's decision-making process. The system allows Soldiers to obtain local situational awareness and understanding of their
 immediate surroundings while remaining in covered or concealed positions. The SBS program will be procured through multiple phases. The program will develop,
 integrate, and qualify additional capabilities for each phase.

 Project A5A - Small Unmanned Aircraft Systems (SUAS) are critical aviation assets that increase lethality and security through organic aerial reconnaissance,
 surveillance, resupply, and targeting capabilities for battalion and below echelons. SUAS enhances situational awareness and force protection for maneuver elements
 operating in complex environments by providing real-time Full Motion Video (FMV) and sensor data through a variety of modular sensors. These multifaceted capabilities
 enable Army formations to leverage the air-ground littoral to project combat power across domains, disrupt near-peer threats, secure key terrain, lighten Soldier load,
 and support mission success through persistent overwatch. Leveraging a Modular Open Systems Approach (MOSA), SUAS platforms are designed for rapid adaptation
 through swappable payloads, advanced autonomous flight capabilities, and scalable software-ensuring relevance against both current and emerging operational threats.

 Project A5B - Uncrewed Vehicle Control (UVC) Scalable Control Interface (SCI) is a software suite that enables Soldiers to control multiple types of uncrewed or
 optionally-manned aircraft and payloads through a unified interface, offering scalable authority levels. It enhances the distribution of Unmanned Aircraft Systems (UAS)
 capabilities by increasing the number of control devices available to Soldiers, Commanders, and Battle Staff, allowing for the simultaneous operation of multiple aircraft
 and payloads from a single control node.

 Project A5C - Unmanned Aircraft Systems (UAS) are vital to multi-domain operations (MDO), enabling ground forces to project power across domains to defeat
 adversaries, secure terrain, and consolidate gains. This includes short and/or vertical takeoff and landing (S/VTOL) Group 4+, Brigade (BDE) and Ultra Endurance-
 UAS (UE-UAS; previously referred to as Long Endurance) platforms. This includes capabilities to support expeditionary, extended range reconnaissance, surveillance,
 target acquisition (RSTA), communications, lethality, and electronic warfare, as well as Human Machine Integrated Formations (HMIF) to refine solutions faster than
 adversaries.


PE 0605345A: Unmanned Aerial Systems Launched Effects...                    UNCLASSIFIED
                                                                                                                                                              Volume 3d - 210
Army                                                                            Page 2 of 48                        R-1 Line #166

===== PAGE 243 =====
                                                                            UNCLASSIFIED
Exhibit R-2, RDT&E Budget Item Justification: PB 2027 Army                                                                                 Date: April 2026
Appropriation/Budget Activity                                                    R-1 Program Element (Number/Name)
2040: Research, Development, Test & Evaluation, Army / BA 5: System              PE 0605345A / Unmanned Aerial Systems Launched Effects Systems Development
Development & Demonstration (SDD)

 Project A5D - Launched Effects (LE) extends the speed, range, lethality and survivability of maneuver formations through the integration of air vehicles, mission systems,
 payloads, and behaviors. LE will provide Army formations with the ability to expedite and strengthening the kill web, facilitate the penetration and dis-integration of the
 enemy's Anti-Access Area Denial (A2AD) environment and extend the maneuver force's reach, lethality, and survivability.

 The UAS Marketplace expands the Defense Industrial Base, expedites capability delivery, and provides warfighters with vetted, flexible solutions to meet mission
 requirements. The UAS Marketplace operationalizes a streamlined acquisition framework for both Programs of Record (PoR) and emerging capabilities, while also
 serving as the mechanism to demonstrate, assess and perform verifications on a range of UAS solutions. The UAS Marketplace empowers the warfighter at the tactical
 edge, providing the flexibility to procure vetted, mission-specific UAS solutions. The PM UAS will leverage this centralized marketplace to execute UAS procurements
 and deliveries, ensuring soldiers are equipped through a modern, efficient, scalable, and capability-validated marketplace.

                                    Prior                             FY 2027      FY 2027      FY 2027                                                 Cost To             Total
      COST ($ in Millions)
                                    Years       FY 2025   FY 2026      Base         OOC          Total     FY 2028     FY 2029     FY 2030     FY 2031 Complete             Cost
A56: Long Range Precision                   -         -      23.169      30.547           -       30.547     46.109       51.054       5.714        5.771         0.000     162.364
Munition (LRPM)
Quantity of RDT&E Articles                  -         -          -           -           -            -          -            -           -            -

A. Mission Description and Budget Item Justification
Long Range Precision Munition (LRPM) is the lethal variant of the Launched Effects (LE) Family of Systems which provide a range of capabilities through variations
of platforms, payloads, and mission systems. LE provides tactical and operational flexibility through collaboration of heterogeneous groups of LE launched from
multiple domains (land and air) in conjunction with other manned platforms, C2, unmanned systems, and sensors throughout the operational environment. The ability
to interoperate and coordinate with other LE systems at long ranges and adapt to changing threats is a core concept of the Launched Effects Abbreviated Capability
Development Document validated in June 2024. The primary target set for LRPM is Integrated Air Defense Systems. LRPM will provide Army Aviation and Ground
Forces with a precise long range munition system to rapidly respond in a combat environment to improve the lethality and stand-off of Warfighters and aviation platforms
in an Anti-Access Area Denial (A2AD) and positioning, navigation, and timing (PNT) denied environment.

 The total cost of the LRPM Middle Tier of Acquisition (MTA) Rapid Prototyping (RP) effort is $122.5 million RDT&E from FY24 to FY29. The LRPM MTA-RP is fully
 funded across the Future Years Defense Program.

B. Accomplishments/Planned Programs ($ in Millions)                                                                                       FY 2025          FY 2026        FY 2027
Title: Long Range Precision Munition                                                                                                             -            23.169         30.547
Description: This line funds the integration and qualification of a LE munition system that will engage and deliver lethal effects
on targets at ranges beyond line of sight. The LRPM Middle Tier of Acquisition (MTA) Rapid Prototyping (RP) effort includes
demonstration and qualification of a precision guided munition with the capability to complete the assigned mission in cyberattack,
countermeasures, and anti-access area denial environments. These efforts will include technical assessments, technology
maturation, test and evaluation, demonstration of prototype hardware, system and platform integration, and document preparation
for associated contract and acquisition efforts.

FY 2026 Plans:
Initiate the system environmental and safety qualification and airworthiness test program for LRPM. Continue LRPM integration of
maturing LE technologies, technical evaluations and program transition planning.
FY 2027 Plans:
Continue the system environmental and safety qualification and airworthiness test program and conduct a ground operational
demonstration for LRPM. Continue LRPM integration of maturing LE technologies, technical valuations, program transition
preparation, and planning, preparation, and initiation of additional capabilities development to increase the LE Family of Systems

D. Acquisition Strategy
The U.S. Army Combat Capabilities Development Command Aviation and Missile Center (DEVCOM AvMC) Other Transaction Authority (OTA) agreement awarded
in July 2024 enables integration of U.S. Army developed software into the LRPM selected air vehicle, development and integration of LRPM specific components,
procurement of fully configured prototype test assets, test and qualification support, and associated deliverables including engineering drawings and test reports through
July 2027. A follow-on Other Transaction Agreement (OTA) to support continued development, delivery and fielding of prototypes, and interim sustainment support will
be awarded in early FY2027. Annual funding will be awarded as necessary to complete the prototyping efforts.

Aerial Unmanned Sys-Eng Dev
Quantity of RDT&E Articles                   -         -          -           -           -            -          -            -           -            -

A. Mission Description and Budget Item Justification
Mobile Brigade Combat Teams (MBCTs) lack adequate organic capabilities at echelon to apply immediate, point, long range, and direct fire effects to destroy tanks,
light armored vehicles, hardened targets, targets in defilade, and personnel targets, while producing minimal collateral damage in complex terrain in all environmental
conditions.

 Development will focus on the Fire Control Stations (FCS), All-Up Round (AUR), and System Architecture. System Architecture will include integrating with Soldier
 Borne Mission command (SBMC), Next Generation Command and Control (NGC2) and complete Universal Vehicle Control (UVC) compliance and integration. Develop
 technology for incorporation into the CDD update, for the enduring program, and conduct tech enhancements from current global conflicts.

 In addition, LASSO is supporting Transformation in Contact (TiC) in support of Indo-Pacific efforts to expand prepositioned stocks and increase deployments and
 exercises to support strategic presence in the region.

 FY 2027 RDT&E funding will be used to conduct evaluations of technologies and capabilities in the loitering munitions capability space, hardware and system
 architecture engineering and integration activities, and testing and evaluation activities. LASSO is awarding up to four hardware contracts to modernize the industrial
 base and generate the domestic ammunition stockpiles.

 The total cost of the Low Altitude Stalking and Strike Ordinance (LASSO) Middle Tier of Acquisition (MTA) effort is $209.4 million RDT&E from FY25 to FY29. This
 includes FY25 Base funding of $15.767 million and mandatory funding of $12.587 million on 0604827A Project LS2.

B. Accomplishments/Planned Programs ($ in Millions)                                                                                        FY 2025          FY 2026        FY 2027
Title: Low Altitude Stalking and Strike Ordnance (LASSO)                                                                                          -            73.292         59.236
Description: LASSO is intended to increase the lethality of the MBCT specifically against tanks, light armored vehicles, hardened
targets, targets in defilade, riverine craft less than 50 tons, and personnel targets.

FY 2026 Plans:
Funding will continue the evaluation of critical capabilities, assess vendor and market maturity, and conduct safety and
qualification testing.
FY 2027 Plans:

D. Acquisition Strategy
The enduring program begins in FY 2025, with a Capability Development Document (CDD) having been approved 30 AUG 2024. The program of record will utilize
Middle Tier of Acquisition (MTA) for Rapid Prototyping for engineering and prototyping efforts. LASSO is awarding up to four hardware contracts to modernize the
industrial base and generate domestic ammunition stockpiles. The LASSO program is utilizing Other Transaction Authority (OTA) and Federal Acquisition Regulation
(FAR) contracts to field critical technologies quickly.

Note
Unmanned Aerial Systems Launched Effects Agile Systems Development is a part of the Department of War Capability Based (Agile) Funding Pilot, which provides
enhanced capabilities by fostering innovation and accelerated deployment of promising technology.


 In FY 2026, this effort will be a continuation from Program Element (PE) 0604827A/FK4 Soldier Borne Sensors (SBS).
 This effort is not a new start.

A. Mission Description and Budget Item Justification
Project A59 - Soldier Borne Sensor (SBS): The SBS is a small unmanned aerial vehicle. The SBS provides a near term solution to three Army War-fighting Challenges
at the Infantry Squad level: develop situational understanding, conduct air-ground reconnaissance, and conduct joint combined arms maneuver. The system is simple
to deploy and use to support the squad leader's decision-making process. The system allows Soldiers to obtain local situational awareness and understanding of their
immediate surroundings while remaining in covered or concealed positions. The SBS program will be procured through multiple phases. The program will develop,
integrate, and qualify additional capabilities for each phase. In FY 2026, this effort will be a continuation from Program Element (PE) 0604827A/FK4 Soldier Borne
Sensors (SBS). This effort is not a new start.

B. Accomplishments/Planned Programs ($ in Millions)                                                                                        FY 2025          FY 2026        FY 2027
Title: Soldier Borne Sensor (SBS)                                                                                                                 -             4.149          4.229
Description: The SBS provides the squad a "quick look" capability providing Situational Awareness (SA).

FY 2026 Plans:
Complete the development, evaluation and testing of a Phase 2 (BH4) variant.
FY 2027 Plans:
Begin the development, evaluation and testing of a Phase 3 (BH4+) variant.
FY 2026 to FY 2027 Increase/Decrease Statement:
Increase due to economic adjustments
                                                                                  Accomplishments/Planned Programs Subtotals                        -           4.149          4.229

Note
Beginning in FY 2026 funding was transferred from Budget Activity (BA) 4, 0604101A Small Unmanned Aerial Vehicle (SUAV) (6.4) and BA 5 0605205A SUAV 6.5 to BA
5 0605345A Unmanned Aerial Systems Launched Effects Systems Development.

A. Mission Description and Budget Item Justification
The Family of Small Uncrewed Aircraft systems are critical to the Army's Transformation in Contact. The Joint Small Uncrewed Aircraft System sUAS Capability
Development Document (J-sUAS CDD) solves current and emergent operational gaps by incorporating Modular Open Systems Approach (MOSA) including swappable
payloads, advanced autonomy and software scalability. These systems provide battalion and below ground maneuver elements with critical situational awareness and
enhanced force protection as well as responsive reconnaissance and targeting capability with real-time Full Motion Video and sensor data. Each system will include
aircraft, ground support equipment, ground control station and Uncrewed Vehicle Control (UVC) software.

 The Short-Range Reconnaissance (SRR) provides organic maneuver platoons an uncrewed air vehicle designed to support Reconnaissance, Surveillance, and Target
 Acquisition (RSTA). The system has an aircraft weight of less than 5 lbs, a range of 3-5 km, and an endurance greater than 30 minutes. The system includes modular
 payloads, obstacle avoidance, target recognition, automated following, and networked capability.

 The Medium Range Reconnaissance (MRR) system, informed by the Company Level Directed Requirement (SUAS DR), provides organic RSTA capability within the
 Maneuver Company of Brigade Combat Teams. The systems will have 10km range and 8-hour endurance. System will include Assured Positioning, Navigation and
 Timing (APNT), Electro-Optical/Infra-Red (EO/IR), laser targeting, and kinetic architectures in a contested environment.

 The Long-Range Reconnaissance (LRR) System provides organic maneuver battalions an uncrewed air vehicle designed to support RSTA efforts. The systems have
 an aircraft weight of less than 55 lbs., a range of 40-60 km and endurance of 5-10 hours. System includes APNT, EO/IR, laser targeting, and kinetic architectures in a
 contested environment.

 The Joint Tactical Autonomous Aerial Resupply System (JTAARS) is an autonomous aerial cargo delivery system, organic to the maneuver commander, that provides
 options for rapid and agile sustainment of highly mobile tactical combat forces, operating in a widely dispersed manner in the tactical support and close areas. JTAARS
 enables maneuver by reducing the tactical force's dependence on ground lines of communication and sustainment, reducing threats to manned convoys and manned
 aerial systems, lightening Soldier load, and shrinking the supply chain. JTAARS provides a lift capability of 125 lbs over 13 km one way (26 km round trip).

 Purpose Built Attritable System (PBAS) low-cost attritable, consumable munition that will provide organic assets to conduct Reconnaissance and Surveillance (R&S) of
 objectives with a reduction in risk to friendly forces, enhanced precision of lethal payload employment, provide maneuver leaders a more accurate disposition of enemy
 forces, and understanding of terrain on the battlefield.

 The UAS Marketplace expands the Defense Industrial Base, expedites capability delivery, and provides warfighters with vetted, flexible solutions to meet mission
 requirements. The UAS Marketplaces operationalizes a streamlined acquisition framework for both Programs of Record (PoR) and emerging capabilities, while also
 serving as the mechanism to demonstrate, assess and perform verifications on a range of UAS solutions. The UAS Marketplace empowers the warfighter at the tactical
 edge, providing the flexibility to procure vetted, mission-specific UAS solutions. The PM UAS will leverage this centralized marketplace to execute UAS procurements
 and deliveries, ensuring soldiers are equipped through a modern, efficient, scalable, and capability-validated marketplace.

 Fiscal Year (FY) 2027 UAS (RDT&E) funding will be utilized to support SUAS System Development, Integration, Demonstration and Experimentation and Test and
 Evaluation efforts and UAS Marketplace.

D. Acquisition Strategy
The Short Range Reconnaissance (SRR) is a Platoon level asset in the Major Capability Acquisition pathway and successfully completed Milestone C in FY2025.
Capability delivery efforts are ongoing in support of Department of War Drone Dominance initiatives and in conjunction with development of Next Generation SRR
requirements.

 The Medium Range Reconnaissance (MRR) is a Company level asset that was informed directly by feedback from Transformation in Contact Units employing
 the systems delivered under the Company Level SUAS Directed Requirement. Capability delivery efforts begin in FY2027 in support of Department of War Drone
 Dominance initiatives.

 The Long Range Reconnaissance Acquisition Shaping Panel (ASP) Acquisition Decision Memorandum (ADM) was approved for Major Capability Acquisition (MCA)
 on 1 Feb 2024. Materiel Development Decision (MDD) Acquisition Decision Memorandum (ADM) was approved for Material Solutions Analysis (MSA) Phase on 15
 Dec 2024. Successful LRR Annex AROC chaired by the Chief of Staff of the Army (CSA) on 19 Dec 2024. On Jun 2025, the Deputy for Acquisition and Systems
 Management (DASM) endorsed executing the program under an Urgent Capability Acquisition (UCA) pathway in parallel to the MCA pathway. On 15 Dec 2025, the
 ADM was approved for Engineering, Manufacturing, and Development (EMD) Phase activities. Prototypes are currently being evaluated from two vendors in two phases
 that include Special User Evaluations (SUE) and technical evaluations.

 The Joint Tactical Autonomous Aerial Resupply System (JTAARS) completed an ASP in fourth quarter FY2023 with direction from the Shaping Panel to conduct
 the FY2024 JTAARS assessment. Following the successful completion of the FY2024 capability assessment, PM UAS returned for a second ASP on 18 December
 2024 and received concurrence to utilize the Urgent Capability Acquisition (UCA) pathway to accelerate the program. JTAARS is currently engaged in operational
 assessments and providing support for Combat Training Center rotations and other Army Exercises.

Note
This is not a new start. In FY 2026, this effort was a realignment from Program Element (PE) 0607143A / EX1 Unmanned Aircraft Systems Universal Products.

A. Mission Description and Budget Item Justification
Uncrewed Vehicle Control (UVC) Scalable Control Interface (SCI) is a permissions-based software control suite used by Soldiers to simultaneously control multiple,
disparate types of uncrewed or optionally-manned aircraft and payloads through a universal interface with scalable levels of authority. SCI distributes UAS capabilities
by greatly enabling more control devices available to Soldiers, Commanders, and Battle Staff. SCI provides simultaneous employment of multiple aircraft/payloads from
a single control node. SCI leverages a Modular Open System Approach (MOSA) to software in order to reduce time and cost to integrate new hardware and software in
response to the dynamic future operating environment.

 Deployment of SCI includes devices in the Mobile/Handheld Computing Environment (such as Nett Warrior), Mounted Computing Environment such as Mounted Family
 of Computer Systems (MFoCS), Command Post Computing Environment such as Tactical Services Infrastructure (TSI), fixed wing aircraft, and rotary wing aircraft. SCI
 will integrate decision aiding, autonomy, and artificial intelligence improvements as they technically mature, in order to support Multi-Domain Operations and reduce
 cognitive workload.

 Fiscal Year (FY) 2027 UAS (RDT&E) funding supports Test and Evaluation efforts for SUAS and software development and system integration of Long Range
 Reconnaissance (LRR) and Launched Effects.

B. Accomplishments/Planned Programs ($ in Millions)                                                                                       FY 2025          FY 2026        FY 2027
Title: Uncrewed Vehicle Control (UVC)                                                                                                            -            33.346         31.340
Description: UVC serves as the primary means of Command and Control (C2) for Program of Record Army UAS. Mission
Command devices host SCI software on both ground and airborne platforms, which act as nodes on the Integrated Tactical
Network to retrieve and provide data. SCI distributes UAS capabilities by significantly increasing the number of UAS control
devices available to Soldiers, Commanders, and Battle Staff. SCI enables simultaneous employment of multiple aircraft and
payloads from a single control node.

FY 2026 Plans:

                                                                                                                                                                  Volume 3d - 237

===== PAGE 270 =====

D. Acquisition Strategy
Project Manager Uncrewed Aircraft Systems (PM UAS) develops the SCI software using a phased acquisition strategy to bridge current systems, integrate future
systems, and evolve UVC capabilities, including artificial intelligence technologies, autonomy, networks, and mature UVC-enabled platforms.

 The SCI meets critical capabilities for UAS command and control. The procedures, infrastructure, developmental environment, and capabilities PM UAS develops for
 SCI establish the foundation for future UAS Command and Control software development, acquisition, and integration into legacy and future platforms.

 PM UAS develops and maintains a product roadmap and product backlog for each of the main capabilities based on the UVC User Agreement. PM UAS gathers user
 feedback through virtual/simulated or live/field test events and uses this feedback to inform prioritization for product roadmaps and backlogs for each capability.

 PM UAS implements software that builds on a Modular Open Systems Approach (MOSA) and aligns with UAS Interoperability Profiles.

 The UAS Marketplace expands the Defense Industrial Base, expedites capability delivery, and provides warfighters with vetted, flexible solutions to meet mission
 requirements. The UAS Marketplace operationalizes a streamlined acquisition framework for both Programs of Record (PoR) and emerging capabilities, while also
 serving as the mechanism to demonstrate, assess, and verify a range of UAS solutions. The UAS Marketplace empowers warfighters at the tactical edge, offering the
 flexibility to procure vetted, mission-specific UAS solutions. PM UAS leverages this centralized marketplace to execute UAS procurements and deliveries, equipping

In FY 2026, this was a realignment from Program Element (PE) 0604113A Future Tactical Unmanned Aircraft System (FTUAS) / EX8 Future Unmanned Aircraft
Systems (FUAS).

 In FY 2027, PE 0609346A/UAS Launched Effects Agile Development, A53 / Unmanned Aircraft System (UAS) will realign to 0605345A - UAS Launched Effects
 Development/A5C - Unmanned Aircraft System (UAS).

A. Mission Description and Budget Item Justification
Unmanned Aircraft Systems (UAS) are critical systems in the cross-domain capabilities concept that will employ multi-domain operation (MDO) capabilities at all
echelons and will allow ground-based forces to project power from land into other domains to defeat highly capable enemies, secure terrain, and consolidate gains.

 Group 4+ UAS S/VTOL provides a short/vertical takeoff and landing, expeditionary, extended range reconnaissance, surveillance, target acquisition (RSTA) with multiple
 sensors, communications, lethality and electromagnetic warfare capability. UAS supports Human Machine Integrated Formations (HMIF) and provides the opportunity to
 learn, fail, refine requirements, and develop solutions faster than our adversaries. UAS incorporates soldier feedback from materiel solution(s) to enhance survivability
 in an MDO environment through modular payloads to facilitate upgraded communication, encryption, and lethality at the pace of technology. UAS provides enhanced
 transportability, rapid deployability, expeditionary maneuver, and mobility for adaptive and agile operations. One system consists of multiple air vehicles with Modular
 Mission Payloads (MMPs), ground control stations, and ancillary equipment. The aircraft subsystem includes the airframe, propulsion system, avionics, communications,
 navigation, and software systems. Aircraft-specific ground support equipment includes power generation, transportation, command and control equipment, aircraft
 software and MMPs.

 Additionally, Ultra Endurance-UAS (UE-UAS; previously referred to as Long Endurance) provide an organic solution capable of long endurance, aerial tier network
 extension with deep sensing and effects needed for highly dispersed, Anti-Access/Area Denial environments. LE-UAS meets Combatant Command persistent
 Intelligence, Surveillance, and Reconnaissance coverage and loiter capability, increased communications relay, and Assured Positioning, Navigation, and timing deep
 sensing and aerial tier network extension critical capability gaps within the Southern border and other requiring COCOMs.

 The Brigade (BDE) UAS initiative will rapidly deliver critical capabilities to Brigade Combat Teams (BCTs) and the Army Transformation and Training Command
 (T2COM), addressing identified gaps in RSTA, communications, lethality, and electronic warfare. Leveraging a readily available VTOL platform with MMPs, the BDE
 UAS provides sustained, multi-role operational capacity in challenging environments.

 The UAS Marketplace expands the Defense Industrial Base, expedites capability delivery, and provides warfighters with vetted, flexible solutions to meet mission
 requirements. The UAS Marketplaces operationalizes a streamlined acquisition framework for both Programs of Record (PoR) and emerging capabilities, while also
 serving as the mechanism to demonstrate, assess and perform verifications on a range of UAS solutions. The UAS Marketplace empowers the warfighter at the tactical
 edge, providing the flexibility to procure vetted, mission-specific UAS solutions. The PM UAS will leverage this centralized marketplace to execute UAS procurements
 and deliveries, ensuring soldiers are equipped through a modern, efficient, scalable, and capability-validated marketplace.

 Fiscal Year (FY) 2027 UAS (RDT&E) funding will be utilized to support BDE, UE-UAS, and Group 4+ UAS S/VTOL.

surveillance, target acquisition (RSTA) with multiple sensors, communications, lethality and electromagnetic warfare capability.

advancements and adversarial threats. These development efforts rely on direct soldier feedback through continuous user
assessments to enhance survivability and effectiveness in any operational environment.

D. Acquisition Strategy
In FY 25 the UAS project office obtained approval to enter an Urgent Capability Acquisition pathway to quickly issue UAS capability, prioritizing mission execution
with cost effective, reusable, and expendable platforms. The BDE UAS platform will be equipped with Multi Mission Payloads, with only ground control stations and
equipment managed by Brigade Combat Teams.

 Ultra Endurance (UE) UAS procure UE-UAS Air Vehicles, payloads, Ground Control Systems, Ground Support Equipment, Launch Equipment, initial spares, Attrition Air
 Vehicles with payloads, Interim Logistics Support, New Equipment Training (NET), and Field Service Representatives to support an Operational Assessments.

 Group 4+ UAS S/VTOL utilizes a Rapid Prototype pathway to develop a prototype system that meets defined performance objectives. The project office employs an
 iterative development process to refine requirements and incorporate user feedback, leveraging modular, scalable designs to enable future upgrades and integration
 with existing systems. This strategy ensures operational relevance and readiness for transition to production within the required timeline. The Abbreviated Capabilities
 Development Document received Army Requirements Oversite Council approval memorandum on 18 November 2025.

 The UAS Marketplace expands the Defense Industrial Base, expedites capability delivery, and provides warfighters with vetted, flexible solutions to meet mission
 requirements. The UAS Marketplace operationalizes a streamlined acquisition framework for both Programs of Record (PoR) and emerging capabilities, while also
 serving as the mechanism to demonstrate, assess and perform verifications on a range of UAS solutions. The UAS Marketplace empowers the warfighter at the tactical
 edge, providing the flexibility to procure vetted, mission-specific UAS solutions. The PM UAS will leverage this centralized marketplace to execute UAS procurements
 and deliveries, ensuring soldiers are equipped through a modern, efficient, scalable, and capability-validated marketplace.

In FY 2026, this was a realignment from Program Element (PE) 0604113A Future Tactical Unmanned Aircraft System (FTUAS) / Project DH3 Air Launched Effects
(ALE).

A. Mission Description and Budget Item Justification
Launched Effects (LE) are a family of air- and ground-launched unmanned systems designed to autonomously or semi-autonomously deliver mission effects-either as
independent agents or as a member of a collaborative networked team.

 LE extends the speed, range, lethality, and survivability of maneuver formations through the integration of air vehicles, mission systems, payloads, and behaviors. LE
 will provide Army formations with the ability to expedite and strengthen the kill web, facilitate the penetration and dis-integration the enemy's Anti-Access Area Denial
 (A2AD) environment and extend the maneuver force's reach, lethality, and survivability. LE efforts are based on requirements from an Army Requirements Oversight
 Council (AROC), approved Abbreviated-Capability Development Document (A-CDD), approved 19 July 2024, and the Army Transformation and Acquisition Reform
 SECDEF memorandum signed 30 April 2025.

 In FY 2026, UAS continued the Urgent Capability Acquisition (UCA) authority requirement and will issue LE capability to every division per 30 April 2025 SECDEF
 memorandum. In FY 2027 LE will respond to high demand signal from EUCOM, PACOM, and other areas of responsibility for LE.

 Manned, optionally-manned, and uncrewed systems will penetrate defense-in-depth environments by employing LE with teaming and swarming effects to detect, decoy,
 jam radar and communications, conduct cyber-attack, spoof and jam Global Positioning System (GPS), and kinetic engagement.

 The UAS Marketplace expands the Defense Industrial Base, expedites capability delivery, and provides warfighters with vetted, flexible solutions to meet mission
 requirements. The UAS Marketplaces operationalizes a streamlined acquisition framework for both Programs of Record (PoR) and emerging capabilities, while also
 serving as the mechanism to demonstrate, assess and perform verifications on a range of UAS solutions. The UAS Marketplace empowers the warfighter at the tactical
 edge, providing the flexibility to procure vetted, mission-specific UAS solutions. The PM UAS will leverage this centralized marketplace to execute UAS procurements
 and deliveries, ensuring soldiers are equipped through a modern, efficient, scalable, and capability-validated marketplace.

B. Accomplishments/Planned Programs ($ in Millions)                                                                                          FY 2025           FY 2026        FY 2027
Title: LE System Development                                                                                                                        -            169.221        172.842

Systems (UAS)

Note
This effort was realigned in FY 2026 from PE 0604741A/FG5 COUNTER UNMANNED AERIAL SYSTEMS (UAS) and continues work previously performed under that
PE.

 The program received $43.300 million of mandatory funding in FY 2026, to support development efforts in response to U.S. Central Command (USCENTCOM) Joint
 Urgent Operational Need (JUON) CC-0587 for elevated airborne sensors to detect, track, and acquire airborne threats.

A. Mission Description and Budget Item Justification
Counter-small Unmanned Aircraft Systems (C-sUAS) funding supports ongoing efforts to provide forces at all echelons with cross-domain capabilities to identify, classify,
track, and defeat Groups 1-3 UAS threats, while supporting joint operational requirements. These combined arms solutions support the full kill-chain and result in
solutions addressing fixed/semi-fixed, mounted/mobile, dismounted, and handheld missions. Development efforts are aligned with the C-sUAS Capability Development
Document (CDD) and Annexes, which codify the threshold and objective capability requirements for C-sUAS development.

 This funding line supports Homeland Defense and is an Air and Missile Defense Army modernization priority.

Quantity of RDT&E Articles                  -         -          -          -           -            -          -            -            -            -

A. Mission Description and Budget Item Justification
The Counter-small Unmanned Aircraft Systems (C-sUAS) FY 2027 capabilities-based funding request will enable innovative cross-domain solutions to sense, decide,
and act against Groups 1-3 UAS threats, while supporting joint operational requirements. These combined arms solutions will support the full kill-chain and result in
solutions addressing fixed/semi-fixed, mounted/mobile platform, dismounted, and handheld missions. Development efforts are aligned with the C-sUAS Capability
Development Document (CDD) and Annexes, which codify the threshold and objective capability requirements for C-sUAS development. This funding supports the
Army's Next Generation Command and Control (NGC2) initiative to modernize Command and Control (C2) systems.

 Fixed/Mobile System Capabilities: Funds rapid component prototyping, facilitates operational assessments, pursues development and integration of mature hardware,
 addresses obsolescence, and tests performance improvements of existing fixed and mobile systems against current and near-term threats. Initiates activities required to
 transition the current mobile C-sUAS solution to field level sustainment.

 Tech Refresh: Funds technology refreshes of C-sUAS capabilities supporting deployed systems, to keep pace with evolving threats in response to Joint Urgent
 Operational Need (JUON) CC-0558, to sense, decide, and act against Groups 1 and 2 UAS threats.

 C-sUAS CDD Pre-Planned Product Improvement (P3I): Funds prototyping, development, and integration of emerging technologies, and testing performance
 improvements against future threats for C-sUAS programs.

 C-sUAS Emergent Requirements: Funds development, testing, integration, and continuous improvement to accelerate emerging C-sUAS capabilities, such as the Next
 Generation C-sUAS Missile (NGCM), Integrated Battle Command System-Maneuver (IBCS-M), C-sUAS non-kinetic interceptor, and Unit Common and Soldier Common
 solutions.

 Test Support for C-sUAS Competitions and Continuous Transformation: Funds additional test requirements, such as targets, range and instrumentation costs, and Other
 Government Agency (OGA) test and evaluation support, for capability set fielding and continuous transformation efforts.

B. Accomplishments/Planned Programs ($ in Millions)                                                                                      FY 2025           FY 2026      FY 2027

B. Accomplishments/Planned Programs ($ in Millions)                                                                                   FY 2025     FY 2026         FY 2027
Description: Funds rapid component prototyping, facilitates operational assessments, pursues development and integration of
mature hardware, addresses obsolescence, and tests performance improvements of existing systems against current and near-
term threats.

FY 2026 Plans:
FY 2026 funding will continue efforts to increase commonality and improve producibility among the CsUAS sensor variants, such
as procuring prototype hardware in support of redesign of line replaceable units (e.g., System Control Unit and Advanced Back-
End Unit), due to obsolescence and multiple components approaching end-of-life. Funding will also support biannual C-sUAS
system-of-systems integration/record tests for new and enhanced components, systems, and subsystems.
FY 2027 Plans:
FY 2027 funding will continue efforts to increase commonality and improve producibility, resulting in cost efficiencies and system
improvements among the C-sUAS sensor variants, such as procuring prototype hardware and conducting prototype integration
and testing in support of redesign of line replaceable units, due to obsolescence and multiple components approaching end-of-
life. Funding will support development and testing of updated technical manuals and safety documentation required to conduct
a Logistics Demonstration for the C-sUAS mobile solution, to initiate transition to field level sustainment. Funding will address
hardware obsolescence and replacement for interceptor system components, resulting in higher operational availability and
a lower cost. Funding will also support biannual C-sUAS system-of-systems integration/record tests for new and enhanced
components, systems, and subsystems.
FY 2026 to FY 2027 Increase/Decrease Statement:
FY 2026 to FY 2027 funding increase is due to the ramp up of efforts in support of transitioning M-LIDS to field level sustainment.
Title: Tech Refresh for Army JUON Efforts                                                                                                    -        6.694           7.089
Description: Funds technology refreshes and continues technological development of C-sUAS capabilities supporting deployed
systems, to keep pace with evolving threats in response to Joint Urgent Operational Need (JUON) CC-0558.

FY 2026 Plans:
FY 2026 Base funding will continue technological development of C-sUAS capabilities supporting deployed systems, such as the
following: fire control and sensor software, improved decision aids to reduce kill chain timelines, software improvements for C-
sUAS radar low altitude tracking and interceptor fire control radar support, etc.
